cd "/c/Users/me/work/ai/promptbook"

if [ -f .env ]; then
set -a
source .env
set +a
fi

unset GITHUB_TOKEN

# Avoid passing the prompt as one CLI argument because large agent prompts can exceed Windows/MSYS limits.
copilot \
    --yolo \
    --no-ask-user \
    --no-color \
    --output-format json \
    --stream off --model gpt-5.4 --reasoning-effort xhigh \
    <<'GITHUB_COPILOT_PROMPT'

Add support for PostgreSQL in Agents server

```bash
root@collboard-agents-server-x21:~# sudo curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/webgptorg/promptbook/refs/heads/main/other/vps/install.sh | bash
```

-   Now the Agents server only supports SQLite and Supabase as the database, add support for PostgreSQL as well
-   When installing Agents server through auto installation script `install.sh`, allow to pick between SQLite and PostgreSQL as the database for the Agents server, by default use PostgreSQL, but if the user prefers to use SQLite, allow them to choose it during the installation process and configure the Agents server to use SQLite instead of PostgreSQL
-   Auto installation script should automatically install and configure PostgreSQL on the server if the user chooses to use PostgreSQL as the database for the Agents server, and also create a new database and user for the Agents server, and configure the Agents server to connect to the PostgreSQL database using the created user and database
-   The database settings should be stored in the `.env` file, and its one of the settings that must be configured during the installation process and cannot be setup during the UI wizzard "Create new server" or in super `admin` UI, but it is allowed just to press enter to use the default PostgreSQL configuration
-   Generate strong random password for the PostgreSQL user and store it in the `.env` file, and also show it to the user during the installation process, so they can save it if they want to access the database directly
-   Both SQLite and PostgreSQL should be supported by the build in Embedded Prisma Studio
-   Keep in mind the DRY _(don't repeat yourself)_ principle also keep in mind that there should be abstracted code for the database connection and the places of the Agents server app which are using the database
-   You are working with the [Agents Server](apps/agents-server)
-   You are working with [auto installation script](vps/install.sh)
-   Add the changes into the [changelog](changelog/_current-preversion.md)

## How to Contribute

-   **Add changes in [Changelog](/changelog/_current-preversion.md)**
-   **Test your changes** to ensure they work as expected, use `npm test`

## Code Style

-   Follow the existing code style and structure.
-   Use comments to explain complex logic.

## Emoji in `[brackets]`

-   `[any emoji]` Connects multiple places that are related to each other across the repository
-   `[number]` Connects multiple places that are related to each other across the file
-   `[🧠]` Marks a place where there is something to decide and think about.
-   `[🕕]` List of models _(that should be progresively updated)_
    -   Prompt: Update available models and their prices, search online
-   `[🔼]` Marks an entity (function, class, type,...) in other project (like Promptbook.studio) which should be moved to this repository
-   `[🚉]` Marks an types / interfaces / structures fully serializable as JSON, not marking `string_` and `number_` prefixed aliases
-   `[🧹]` Need to implement garbage collection
-   `[🐣]` Easter eggs
-   `[💩]` Shitty code that needs refactoring
-   `$` When entity (function, class) starts by `$`, it means it is not pure and can have side effects.
-   3x `!` Marks a place that needs to be fixed before releasing a pre-release version.
-   4x (and more) `!` Marks a place that needs to be fixed as soon as possible.
-   `@@@` Marks a place where text / documentation / ... must be written.
-   [⚫] Code in this file should never be published in any package
-   [🟢] Code in this file should never be published into packages that could be imported into browser environment
-   [🔵] Code in this file should never be published outside of `@promptbook/browser`
-   [🟡] Code in this file should never be published outside of `@promptbook/cli`
-   [💞] Ignore a discrepancy between file name and entity name

## Generated code

Across the repository, there are several places where code has been generated automatically.
Do not edit these places manually, as they will be overwritten by the code generation process. Edit the source files instead.

> ⚠️ WARNING: This code has been generated so that any manual changes will be overwritten

## Dictionary

-   **Promptbook Engine vs. Agent Server** sre two distinct parts you should know the difference:
    -   **Promptbook Engine** is the core engine that powers AI agents, it is located in `/src` folder. It is framework-agnostic and can be used in different applications. It can run as standalone javascript/typescript library, CLI environment, or be integrated into different applications. It can be also runned in Agent Server:
    -   **Agent Server** is a specific application that uses the Promptbook Engine to provide a web and API interface for creating and interacting with AI agents. It is located in `/apps/agents-server` folder.
-   **Commitments** are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents
    -   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, etc.
    -   Commitments are in the folder `/src/commitments`
    -   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g., `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, etc. on a begining of the line and end by new co
    -   Agent source with commitments is parsed by two functions:
        -   `parseAgentSource` which is a lightweight parser for agent source, it parses basic information and its purpose is to be quick and synchronous. The commitments there are hardcoded.
        -   `createAgentModelRequirements` which is an asynchronous function that creates model requirements it applies each commitment one by one and works asynchronously.

## Common rules

-   Always analyze the context and requirements before generating any code.
-   Write clear, maintainable, and well-documented code.
-   Write JSDoc comments for all entities - functions, classes, types, top-level constants, etc.
    -   When this entity is exported from the file and it is under `src` folder _(not for example in the `apps` folder)_, it must be marked either as `@public` or `@private` at the end of the JSDoc comment.
    -   For example: "@private internal utility of <Chat/>" / "@public exported from `@promptbook/browser`"
    -   If you don't know, prefer to mark it as private, we can always change it to public later, but changing from public to private may cause breaking changes.
-   You (the AI coding agent) are running inside a Node process, so do not kill all Node processes such as `taskkill /F /IM node.exe`. If you need to stop something you spawned, kill only that specific process, for example by PID or by port.

## Additional context

-   Attached images (if any) are relative to the root of the project.

### The Agents Server menu _(as additional context)_

The menu of the agent server looks like this:

1. The navigation hierarchy
    - Icon and Server name _(for example Promptbook Agents Server)_
    - arrow ">" and Agents or picked agent name (organized in folders)
    - arrow ">" and the view Profile / Chat / Book of the agent or nothing if no agent is picked
2. The menu items
    - Documentation
    - System
3. Control panel and user menu
    - Control panel
    - User menu with the avatar and the name of the user

### Database migrations for Agents server _(as additional context)_

-   Migrations are located in `apps/agents-server/src/database/migrations`
-   Be aware that table names in migrations have prefix `prefix_` _(look at existing migrations for reference)_
-   Migrations should be backwards compatible:
    -   Meaning that earlier versions of the server should be able to run with the database after migration without any issues.
    -   This is important because we want to have the same database for production and preview environments.
    -   This database will be migrated to the latest version of the preview environment, but the production environment should be able to run with it without any issues.
    -   The only thing that can happen is that older versions of the server will not be able to use new features.
    -   Adding new columns, tables, etc. is fine, but do not remove or rename existing ones, and do not change the meaning of existing columns or tables.
    -   When in doubt, prefer to add new things instead of changing existing ones.

### Metadata of Agents server _(as additional context)_

-   There is a table called `Metadata`.
-   It has `key` and `value` fields.
-   It is a similar concept to configuration, but this configuration can be changed by administrators in the Agents Server website.

### Book Language blueprint _(as additional context)_

Book language is a domain-specific language used for defining AI agents in the Promptbook Engine and Agents server.
It has lightweight syntax and keywords (the commitments) that allow you to define the "soul" of the agent.
The book language is designed to be human-readable and easy to write, while also being powerful enough to express complex agent behaviors.

Every agent has its source defined in the book language, which is called "agent source". The agent source is parsed and processed by the Promptbook Engine to create the actual AI agent that can interact with users and perform tasks.
This agent source is internally converted to a structured format called "agent model requirements" which are the actual raw technical instructions for the AI model to run the agent.

There is a standalone book language documentation on each agent server on `/api/docs/book.md` route, for example `https://pavol-hejny.ptbk.io/api/docs/book.md`.
Use it as a reference for the syntax and semantics of the book language, and modify `apps/agents-server/src/utils/bookLanguageDocumentation/createStandaloneBookLanguageMarkdown.ts` if it is relevant to the change you are doing.

#### Commitments _(as additional context and part of Book Language)_

Commitments are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents written in `book` language.

-   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, `CLOSED`, etc.
-   They are in the folder `src/commitments`.
-   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g. `PERSONA`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, etc. on a beginning of the line and ends by a new commitment or the end of the book.
-   There is a general pattern that the commitment keyword is followed by a space and then by the content of the commitment, for example:
    -   `PERSONA You are a helpful assistant that helps with cooking recipes.`
    -   `USE SEARCH ENGINE Search only in French.`
-   In the commitment context, you can reference external agents, for example:
    -   `TEAM You can talk to {Criminal lawyer} and {Financial advisor}`

## Coding rules

-   Keep in mind the DRY _(don't repeat yourself)_ principle.
-   Keep in mind the SOLID principles.
-   Do a proper analysis of the current functionality before you start implementing.
-   Keep small responsibilities of functions and classes, avoid creating big functions or classes that do many things.
-   When throwing errors, throw [branded errors](src/errors) and use `spaceTrim` utility to write clear and well-formatted multiline detailed error messages.
    -   Format errors as markdown, for example `variables` should be in backticks and important notes can be in bold.
-   Constants should always be `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`.
-   Boolean variables should always be prefixed with `is`, for example `isUserChatJobLeaseExpired` or `IS_DEBUG_MODE`.
-   Do not use abbreviations, for example use `isExpired` instead of `isExp`, `translateMessage` instead of `t`, etc.
    -   It is fine to use well-known abbreviations, for example `id`, `url`, `html`, etc.
-   When writing multiline strings, use `spaceTrim` utility.
-   Do only the change described in the prompt. Do not add any additional features or make any additional changes that are not described there.
    -   If you find some critical issue that is not described in the prompt, report it to `./AGENT_REPORT.md` in the root of the project.

file\n-   `[🧠]` Marks a place where there is something to decide and think about.\n-   `[🕕]` List of models _(that should be progresively updated)_\n    -   Prompt: Update available models and their prices, search online\n-   `[🔼]` Marks an entity (function, class, type,...) in other project (like Promptbook.studio) which should be moved to this repository\n-   `[🚉]` Marks an types / interfaces / structures fully serializable as JSON, not marking `string_` and `number_` prefixed aliases\n-   `[🧹]` Need to implement garbage collection\n-   `[🐣]` Easter eggs\n-   `[💩]` Shitty code that needs refactoring\n-   `$` When entity (function, class) starts by `$`, it means it is not pure and can have side effects.\n-   3x `!` Marks a place that needs to be fixed before releasing a pre-release version.\n-   4x (and more) `!` Marks a place that needs to be fixed as soon as possible.\n-   `@@@` Marks a place where text / documentation / ... must be written.\n-   [⚫] Code in this file should never be published in any package\n-   [🟢] Code in this file should never be published into packages that could be imported into browser environment\n-   [🔵] Code in this file should never be published outside of `@promptbook/browser`\n-   [🟡] Code in this file should never be published outside of `@promptbook/cli`\n-   [💞] Ignore a discrepancy between file name and entity name\n\n## Generated code\n\nAcross the repository, there are several places where code has been generated automatically.\nDo not edit these places manually, as they will be overwritten by the code generation process. Edit the source files instead.\n\n> ⚠️ WARNING: This code has been generated so that any manual changes will be overwritten\n\n## Dictionary\n\n-   **Promptbook Engine vs. Agent Server** sre two distinct parts you should know the difference:\n    -   **Promptbook Engine** is the core engine that powers AI agents, it is located in `/src` folder. It is framework-agnostic and can be used in different applications. It can run as standalone javascript/typescript library, CLI environment, or be integrated into different applications. It can be also runned in Agent Server:\n    -   **Agent Server** is a specific application that uses the Promptbook Engine to provide a web and API interface for creating and interacting with AI agents. It is located in `/apps/agents-server` folder.\n-   **Commitments** are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents\n    -   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, etc.\n    -   Commitments are in the folder `/src/commitments`\n    -   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g., `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, etc. on a begining of the line and end by new co\n    -   Agent source with commitments is parsed by two functions:\n        -   `parseAgentSource` which is a lightweight parser for agent source, it parses basic information and its purpose is to be quick and synchronous. The commitments there are hardcoded.\n        -   `createAgentModelRequirements` which is an asynchronous function that creates model requirements it applies each commitment one by one and works asynchronously.\n\n## Common rules\n\n-   Always analyze the context and requirements before generating any code.\n-   Write clear, maintainable, and well-documented code.\n-   Write JSDoc comments for all entities - functions, classes, types, top-level constants, etc.\n    -   When this entity is exported from the file and it is under `src` folder _(not for example in the `apps` folder)_, it must be marked either as `@public` or `@private` at the end of the JSDoc comment.\n    -   For example: \"@private internal utility of <Chat/>\" / \"@public exported from `@promptbook/browser`\"\n    -   If you don't know, prefer to mark it as private, we can always change it to public later, but changing from public to private may cause breaking changes.\n-   You (the AI coding agent) are running inside a Node process, so do not kill all Node processes such as `taskkill /F /IM node.exe`. If you need to stop something you spawned, kill only that specific process, for example by PID or by port.\n\n## Additional context\n\n-   Attached images (if any) are relative to the root of the project.\n\n### The Agents Server menu _(as additional context)_\n\nThe menu of the agent server looks like this:\n\n1. The navigation hierarchy\n    - Icon and Server name _(for example Promptbook Agents Server)_\n    - arrow \">\" and Agents or picked agent name (organized in folders)\n    - arrow \">\" and the view Profile / Chat / Book of the agent or nothing if no agent is picked\n2. The menu items\n    - Documentation\n    - System\n3. Control panel and user menu\n    - Control panel\n    - User menu with the avatar and the name of the user\n\n### Database migrations for Agents server _(as additional context)_\n\n-   Migrations are located in `apps/agents-server/src/database/migrations`\n-   Be aware that table names in migrations have prefix `prefix_` _(look at existing migrations for reference)_\n-   Migrations should be backwards compatible:\n    -   Meaning that earlier versions of the server should be able to run with the database after migration without any issues.\n    -   This is important because we want to have the same database for production and preview environments.\n    -   This database will be migrated to the latest version of the preview environment, but the production environment should be able to run with it without any issues.\n    -   The only thing that can happen is that older versions of the server will not be able to use new features.\n    -   Adding new columns, tables, etc. is fine, but do not remove or rename existing ones, and do not change the meaning of existing columns or tables.\n    -   When in doubt, prefer to add new things instead of changing existing ones.\n\n### Metadata of Agents server _(as additional context)_\n\n-   There is a table called `Metadata`.\n-   It has `key` and `value` fields.\n-   It is a similar concept to configuration, but this configuration can be changed by administrators in the Agents Server website.\n\n### Book Language blueprint _(as additional context)_\n\nBook language is a domain-specific language used for defining AI agents in the Promptbook Engine and Agents server.\nIt has lightweight syntax and keywords (the commitments) that allow you to define the \"soul\" of the agent.\nThe book language is designed to be human-readable and easy to write, while also being powerful enough to express complex agent behaviors.\n\nEvery agent has its source defined in the book language, which is called \"agent source\". The agent source is parsed and processed by the Promptbook Engine to create the actual AI agent that can interact with users and perform tasks.\nThis agent source is internally converted to a structured format called \"agent model requirements\" which are the actual raw technical instructions for the AI model to run the agent.\n\nThere is a standalone book language documentation on each agent server on `/api/docs/book.md` route, for example `https://pavol-hejny.ptbk.io/api/docs/book.md`.\nUse it as a reference for the syntax and semantics of the book language, and modify `apps/agents-server/src/utils/bookLanguageDocumentation/createStandaloneBookLanguageMarkdown.ts` if it is relevant to the change you are doing.\n\n#### Commitments _(as additional context and part of Book Language)_\n\nCommitments are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents written in `book` language.\n\n-   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, `CLOSED`, etc.\n-   They are in the folder `src/commitments`.\n-   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g. `PERSONA`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, etc. on a beginning of the line and ends by a new commitment or the end of the book.\n-   There is a general pattern that the commitment keyword is followed by a space and then by the content of the commitment, for example:\n    -   `PERSONA You are a helpful assistant that helps with cooking recipes.`\n    -   `USE SEARCH ENGINE Search only in French.`\n-   In the commitment context, you can reference external agents, for example:\n    -   `TEAM You can talk to {Criminal lawyer} and {Financial advisor}`\n\n## Coding rules\n\n-   Keep in mind the DRY _(don't repeat yourself)_ principle.\n-   Keep in mind the SOLID principles.\n-   Do a proper analysis of the current functionality before you start implementing.\n-   Keep small responsibilities of functions and classes, avoid creating big functions or classes that do many things.\n-   When throwing errors, throw [branded errors](src/errors) and use `spaceTrim` utility to write clear and well-formatted multiline detailed error messages.\n    -   Format errors as markdown, for example `variables` should be in backticks and important notes can be in bold.\n-   Constants should always be `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`.\n-   Boolean variables should always be prefixed with `is`, for example `isUserChatJobLeaseExpired` or `IS_DEBUG_MODE`.\n-   Do not use abbreviations, for example use `isExpired` instead of `isExp`, `translateMessage` instead of `t`, etc.\n    -   It is fine to use well-known abbreviations, for example `id`, `url`, `html`, etc.\n-   When writing multiline strings, use `spaceTrim` utility.\n-   Do only the change described in the prompt.   Changed the standalone VPS installer to use the Promptbook GitHub repository for `ptbk agents-server` instead of installing `ptbk` from the npm registry:\n \n     -   `other/vps/install.sh` now keeps a managed checkout of `https://github.com/webgptorg/promptbook.git`, installs its dependencies with `npm ci --include=dev`, and writes a local `ptbk` launcher used by init, build, and pm2 startup.\n     -   Kept npm package publishing and runner CLI installation unchanged; only the VPS auto-install path now avoids the Promptbook npm package dependency.\n \n -   Fixed standalone VPS installation prompts so custom domain collection and DNS instructions appear before system package and runner installation starts.\n \n -   Added standalone VPS installer prompts for `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `ADMIN_PASSWORD`, keeping the API key empty by default and preserving the generated admin-password fallback when the password prompt is left blank.\n \n -   Added production custom-domain setup to the standalone VPS installer:\n \n     -   `other/vps/install.sh` now asks for one or more domains, stores them in `SERVERS`, configures nginx as the public reverse proxy, binds the Agents Server process to localhost, opens HTTP/HTTPS when `ufw` is active, and provisions Let's Encrypt certificates with certbot.\n     -   Added `SERVERS` runtime support for standalone Agents Server deployments so each configured domain resolves to its own deterministic table prefix in the shared database.\n     -   The installer now prints the DNS records that must point at the VPS before certificate issuance and summarizes nginx, certbot, pm2, and admin-login follow-up commands after installation.\n     -   Fixed local SQLite update handling for auto-increment tables so standalone management API writes, including agent ownership assignment, persist correctly.\n \n -   Fixed standalone VPS installation of `ptbk agents-server` so the first production build no longer crashes when the bundled Next app comes from the npm-installed CLI package:\n \n     -   Materialized the packaged Agents Server runtime into the launch project's `.promptbook/agents-server/runtime` cache before building, keeping writable build output out of `node_modules` and preserving access to the installed dependency tree.\n     -   Added explicit Next aliases and packaged CLI dependencies needed by the Agents Server app build, including the React version used by the app.\n     -   Changed `other/vps/install.sh` to run `ptbk agents-server build` before registering the pm2 process, and made the build command load the project `.env`, so install-time build failures stop the installer instead of creating a pm2 restart loop.\n \n -   Added standalone VPS installation support for `ptbk agents-server`:\n \n     -   Added `other/vps/install.sh`, an idempotent Ubuntu 24.04 x64 installer that installs system dependencies, Node.js 22, `ptbk`, `pm2`, the selected coding runner, configures `.env`, opens the configured port when `ufw` is active, and runs `ptbk agents-server start --agent github-copilot --model gpt-5.4 --thinking-level xhigh` under `pm2` with boot startup.\n     - 'wait',\n     };\n     const perPrefix: Array<DatabaseMigrationPrefixResult> = [];\n     let client: Client | null = null;\n     let selectedPrefixes: Array<string> = [];\n     let totalMigrationFiles = 0;\n     let hasExecutionLock = false;\n     let isSkippedDueToActiveMigrationLock = false;\n     let migrationFailure: unknown = null;\n     let migrationsDirectory = '';\n     let migrationFiles: Array<string> = [];\n \n     try {\n         selectedPrefixes = selectPrefixesForMigration(options.prefixes, options.registeredServers ?? 'wait');\n \n         if (!hasExecutionLock) {\n             isSkippedDueToActiveMigrationLock = true;\n \n             return {\n                 processedPrefixes: [],\n                 totalMigrationFiles,\n                 perPrefix,\n                 isSkippedDueToActiveMigrationLock,\n             };\n         }\n \n         for (const prefix of selectedPrefixes) {\n             failureContext.stage = 'migrate-prefix';\n             failureContext.currentPrefix = prefix;\n             failureContext.currentPrefixStage = undefined;\n             failureContext.currentMigrationFile = undefined;\n             logger.info(`\\n🏗️ Migrating prefix: \"${prefix}\"`);\n             const appliedCount = await migratePrefix({\n                 prefix,\n                 appliedBy: options.appliedBy,\n                 manualAppliedByDefault: DATABASE_MIGRATION_APPLIED_BY.MANUAL,\n                 client,\n                 logger,\n                 migrationFiles,\n                 migrationsDirectory,\n                 logSqlStatements: options.logSqlStatements ?? false,\n                 onProgress: ({ prefix, stage, migrationFile }) => {\n                     failureContext.currentPrefix = prefix;\n                     failureContext.currentPrefixStage = stage;\n                     failureContext.currentMigrationFile = migrationFile;\n                 },\n             });\n             perPrefix.push({ prefix, appliedCount });\n         }\n \n         return {\n             processedPrefixes: selectedPrefixes,\n             totalMigrationFiles,\n             perPrefix,\n             isSkippedDueToActiveMigrationLock,\n         };\n     } catch (error) {\n         migrationFailure = createDatabaseMigrationFailureError(error, failureContext);\n     } finally {\n         if (client) {\n             if (hasExecutionLock) {\n                 await releaseMigrationExecutionLock(client, logger);\n             }\n \n             try {\n                 await client.end();\n             } catch (clientEndError) {\n                 if (migrationFailure === null) {\n                     migrationFailure = clientEndError;\n                 } else {\n                     logger.warn('⚠️ Failed to close migration database connection');\n                     logger.warn(String(clientEndError));\n                 }\n             }\n         }\n"},"toolTelemetry":{"properties":{"command":"view","options":"{\"truncateBasedOn\":\"tokenCount\",\"truncateStyle\":\"middle\"}","inputs":"[\"path\",\"view_range\",\"command\"]","resolvedPathAgainstCwd":"false","fileExtension":"[\".ts\"]","viewType":"file"},"metrics":{"resultLength":12535,"resultForLlmLength":12535,"responseTokenLimit":68000},"restrictedProperties":{}}},"id":"4212b871-60c6-49ae-aa96-cc9dff2ddcf4","timestamp":"2026-05-28T18:25:06.975Z","parentId":"84627db4-473b-4ecb-90da-642d70291899"}

'PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_SQLITE_PATH';\n \n /**\n  * Optional hostname used by the internal Next server.\n  *\n  * @private internal constant of `ptbk agents-server`\n  */\n const PTBK_HOSTNAME_ENV = export type StartAgentsServerOptions = {\n     readonly port: number_port;\n     readonly agentName: PromptRunnerAgentName;\n     readonly model?: string;\n     readonly noUi: boolean;\n     readonly thinkingLevel?: ThinkingLevel;\n     readonly allowCredits: boolean;\n     readonly isBuildForced: boolean;\n };\n \n /**\n  * Captures foreground file locations for one Agents Server launch.\n  *\n  * @private internal type of `ptbk agents-server`\n  */\n type AgentsServerRuntimePaths = {\n     readonly launchWorkingDirectory: string;\n     readonly appPath: string;\n     readonly agentRootPath: string;\n     readonly logDirectoryPath: string;\n     readonly nextLogPath: string;\n     readonly runnerLogPath: string;\n };\n \n /**\n  * Logger handles kept open for the foreground Agents Server lifetime.\n  *\n  * @private internal type of `ptbk agents-server`\n  */\n type AgentsServerLogStreams = {\n     readonly next: WriteStream;\n     readonly runner: WriteStream;\n };\n \n /**\n  * Subprocess environment carrying the explicit local worker credential.\n  *\n  * @private internal type of `ptbk agents-server`\n  */\n type AgentsServerChildEnvironment = NodeJS.ProcessEnv & {\n     readonly PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_USER_CHAT_WORKER_TOKEN: string;\n };\n \n /**\n  * Mutable supervisor state shared with child-process and watcher callbacks.\n  *\n  * @private internal type of `ptbk agents-server`\n  */\n type AgentsServerSupervisorState = {\n     isContinuing: boolean;\n     uiHandle?: CoderRunUiHandle;\n     lastUserChatJobWorkerError?: {\n         message: string;\n         repeatCount: number;\n     };\n     nextExit?: {\n         code: number | null;\n         signal: NodeJS.Signals | null;\n     };\n };\n \n /**\n  * Starts the Agents Server web app and local coding-agent queue workers in the foreground.\n  *\n  * @private internal utility of `ptbk agents-server`\n  */\n export async function startAgentsServer(options: StartAgentsServerOptions): Promise<void> {\n     const runtimePaths = await resolveAgentsServerRuntimePaths();\n     loadAgentsServerProjectEnvironment(runtimePaths.launchWorkingDirectory);\n     await Promise.all([\n         mkdir(runtimePaths.agentRootPath, { recursive: true }),\n         mkdir(runtimePaths.logDirectoryPath, { recursive: true }),\n     ]);\n \n     const logStreams = createAgentsServerLogStreams(runtimePaths);\n     const state: AgentsServerSupervisorState = { isContinuing: true };\n     const childEnvironment = createAgentsServerChildEnvironment(options.port, runtimePaths.agentRootPath);\n     let nextServerProcess: ChildProcess | undefined;\n     let stopUserChatJobWorkerPump: (() => void) | undefined;\n \n     logRunnerEvent(logStreams.runner, `Launching from ${runtimePaths.launchWorkingDirectory}.`);\n     logRunnerEvent(logStreams.runner, `Agent folders: ${runtimePaths.agentRootPath}.`);\n     logRunnerEvent(logStreams.runner, `Agents Server URL: http://localhost:${options.port}.`);\n \n     const processExitHandler = (): void => {\n         stopChildProcess(nextServerProcess);\n     };\n     process.once('exit', processExitHandler);\n \n     try {\n         const buildArtifacts = await ensureAgentsServerBuild({\n             appPath: runtimePaths.appPath,\n             environment: childEnvironment,\n             isBuildForced: options.isBuildForced,\n             onBuildEvent: (event) => {\n                 logRunnerEvent(logStreams.runner, event);\n                 forwardChildOutput(`${event}\\n`, {\n });\n         const runtimeChildEnvironment = createAgentsServerRuntimeEnvironment(\n             childEnvironment,\n             buildArtifacts.nodeModulesPath,\n         ) as environment: runtimeChildEnvironment,\n             logStreams,\n             state,\n         });\n \n         await withCurrentWorkingDirectory(runtimePaths.agentRootPath, async () => {\n             await runMultipleAgentMessages(createLocalAgentRunOptions(options), {\n                 shouldContinue: () => state.isContinuing,\n                 watchErrorLogDirectoryPath: runtimePaths.logDirectoryPath,\n                 onUiInitialized: (uiHandle) => {\n                     state.uiHandle = uiHandle;\n                     addUiOutput(\n                         state,\n                         `Agents Server running at http://localhost:${options.port}. Next logs: ${runtimePaths.nextLogPath}`,\n                     );\n                 },\n             });\n         });\n \n         assertNextServerStillRunning(state);\n     } finally {\n         state.isContinuing = false;\n         stopUserChatJobWorkerPump?.();\n         stopChildProcess(nextServerProcess);\n         process.off('exit', processExitHandler);\n         closeAgentsServerLogStreams(logStreams);\n     }\n }\n \n /**\n  * Loads launch-directory `.env` values without overriding explicit process environment.\n  *\n  * @private internal utility of `ptbk agents-server`\n  */\n export function loadAgentsServerProjectEnvironment(launchWorkingDirectory: string): void {\n     dotenv.config({ path: join(launchWorkingDirectory, AGENTS_SERVER_PROJECT_ENV_FILE_NAME) });\n }\n \n /**\n  * Resolves local app, agent-root, and log paths for one foreground launch.\n  */\n async function resolveAgentsServerRuntimePaths(): Promise<AgentsServerRuntimePaths> {\n     const launchWorkingDirectory = process.cwd();\n     const appPath = await resolveAgentsServerAppPath();\n     const logDirectoryPath = join(launchWorkingDirectory, AGENTS_SERVER_LOG_DIRECTORY_NAME);\n \n     return {\n         launchWorkingDirectory,\n         appPath,\n         agentRootPath: resolvePromptbookTemporaryPath(launchWorkingDirectory, 'agents-server', 'agents'),\n         logDirectoryPath,\n         nextLogPath: join(logDirectoryPath, 'agents-server-next.log'),\n         runnerLogPath: join(logDirectoryPath, 'agents-server-runner.log'),\n     };\n }\n \n /**\n  * Starts the production Next server and wires its logs into the foreground dashboard.\n  */\n function startNextServer(options: {\n     readonly nextCliPath: string;\n     readonly options: StartAgentsServerOptions;\n     readonly runtimePaths: AgentsServerRuntimePaths;\n     readonly childEnvironment: NodeJS.ProcessEnv;\n     readonly logStreams: AgentsServerLogStreams;\n     readonly state: AgentsServerSupervisorState;\n }): ChildProcess {\n     logRunnerEvent(options.logStreams.runner, 'Starting the Agents Server Next process.');\n     const nextArguments = [options.nextCliPath, 'start', '--port', String(options.options.port)];\n     const hostname = options.childEnvironment[PTBK_HOSTNAME_ENV]?.trim();\n \n     if (hostname) {\n         nextArguments.push('--hostname', hostname);\n         logRunnerEvent(options.logStreams.runner, `Binding Agents Server Next process to ${hostname}.`);\n     }\n \n     const commandProcess = spawn(process.execPath, nextArguments, {\n         cwd: options.runtimePaths.appPath,\n         env: options.childEnvironment,\n         stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],\n     });\n \n     bindChildOutput(commandProcess, {\n         label: 'next',\n         logStream: options.logStreams.next,\n         state: options.state,\n     });\n \n     commandProcess.once('exit', (code, signal) => {\n         options.state.nextExit = { code, signal };\n         options.state.isContinuing = false;\n         logRunnerEvent(\n             options.logStreams.runner,\n             `Agents Server Next process exited with code ${String(code)} and signal ${String(signal)}.`,\n         );\n     });\n \n     commandProcess.once('error', (error) => {\n         options.state.isContinuing = false;\n         logRunnerEvent(options.logStreams.runner, `Agents Server Next process failed: ${error.message}`);\n         addUiError(options.state, error.message);\n     });\n \n     return commandProcess;\n }\n \n /**\n  * Connects child stdout/stderr to the persisted Next log and visible terminal output.\n  */\n function bindChildOutput(\n     commandProcess: ChildProcess,\n     options: {\n         readonly label: string;\n         readonly logStream: WriteStream;\n         readonly state: AgentsServerSupervisorState;\n     },\n ): void {\n     commandProcess.stdout?.on('data', (chunk) => {\n         forwardChildOutput(chunk.toString(), options);\n     });\n     commandProcess.stderr?.on('data', (chunk) => {\n         forwardChildOutput(chunk.toString(), options);\n     });\n }\n \n /**\n  * Persists one child output chunk and forwards readable lines into UI AgentsServerSupervisorState;\n     },\n ): void {\n     options.logStream.write(chunk);\n \n     const visibleLines = chunk\n         .split(/\\r?\\n/gu)\n         .map((line) => line.trimEnd())\n         .filter((line) => line.trim().length > 0);\n \n     for (const line of visibleLines) {\n         const labeledLine = `[${options.label}] ${line}`;\n         if (options.state.uiHandle) {\n // Next loads app-local `.env` values after the CLI has prepared this bridge environment.\n         PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_USER_CHAT_WORKER_TOKEN:\n   message,\n                     state: options.state,\n                 });\n             })\n             .finally(() => {\n                 isTickRunning = false;\n             });\n     }, Raw database or request-host value.\n  * @returns Normalized host or `host:port`, or `null` when invalid.\n  */\n export function normalizeServerDomain(rawDomain: string): string | null {\n     const trimmedDomain = rawDomain.trim();\n     if (!trimmedDomain) {\n         return null;\n     }\n \n     const candidateUrl = hasHttpProtocol(trimmedDomain) ? trimmedDomain : `https://${trimmedDomain}`;\n \n     try {\n         const parsedUrl = new URL(candidateUrl);\n         const normalizedHostname = parsedUrl.hostname.trim().toLowerCase();\n         if (!normalizedHostname) {\n             return null;\n         }\n \n         if (parsedUrl.port && !isDefaultPortForProtocol(parsedUrl.protocol, parsedUrl.port)) {\n             return `${normalizedHostname}:${parsedUrl.port}`;\n         }\n \n         return normalizedHostname;\n     } catch {\n         return null;\n     }\n"},"toolTelemetry":{"properties":{"command":"view","options":"{\"truncateBasedOn\":\"tokenCount\",\"truncateStyle\":\"middle\"}","inputs":"[\"path\",\"view_range\",\"command\"]","resolvedPathAgainstCwd":"false","fileExtension":"[\".ts\"]","viewType":"file"},"metrics":{"resultLength":3579,"resultForLlmLength":3579,"responseTokenLimit":68000},"restrictedProperties":{}}},"id":"962bb8b6-2a54-4901-aac7-e2dbe16f75fa","timestamp":"2026-05-28T18:25:27.838Z","parentId":"327142b9-9122-42c4-a260-63a2eff85626"}

`userChat`\n  */\n function createUserChatSummarySeedFromChatRecord(chat: UserChatRecord): UserChatSummarySeed {\n     return {\n         id: chat.id,\n         createdAt: chat.createdAt,\n         updatedAt: chat.updatedAt,\n         lastMessageAt: chat.lastMessageAt,\n         title: chat.title,\n         source: chat.source,\n         messagesCount: chat.messages.length,\n         firstUserMessageContent: resolveFirstUserMessageContent(chat.messages),\n         lastPreviewMessageContent: resolveLastPreviewMessageContent(chat.messages),\n         pendingAssistantMessageCount: countPendingAssistantMessages(chat.messages),\n     };\n }\n \n /**\n  * Resolves first user-authored message content for chat title derivation.\n  *\n  * @private function of `userChat`\n  */\n function resolveFirstUserMessageContent(messages: ReadonlyArray<UserChatRecord['messages'][number]>): string {\n     const firstUserMessage = messages.find((message) => String(message.sender || '').toUpperCase() === 'USER');\n     return normalizeMessageContent(firstUserMessage?.content);\n }\n \n /**\n  * Resolves latest non-empty message content used as sidebar preview text.\n  *\n  * @private function of `userChat`\n  */\n function resolveLastPreviewMessageContent(messages: ReadonlyArray<UserChatRecord['messages'][number]>): string {\n     for (let index = messages.length - 1; index >= 0; index--) {\n         const normalizedContent = normalizeMessageContent(messages[index]?.content);\n         if (normalizedContent) {\n             return normalizedContent;\n         }\n Agents Server is the main web application where Promptbook agents live\n4. \n5. ## Local CLI configuration\n6. \n7. The installed CLI can initialize a local Agents Server project before it starts the packaged web app:\n8. \n9. ```bash\n10. npm install ptbk\n11. ptbk agents-server init\n12. ptbk agents-server start --agent github-copilot --model gpt-5.4 --thinking-level xhigh\n13. ```\n14. \n15. `ptbk agents-server init` adds missing placeholders to `.env` and local runtime exclusions to `.gitignore` without deleting existing configuration. Use `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_DATABASE=supabase` for a Supabase-backed server or `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_DATABASE=sqlite` for a standalone local database in `.promptbook`. When using Supabase, fill the initialized values from your project before starting the server. The Supabase project URL and API keys are available in the [Supabase project API settings](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/api/api-keys), and the PostgreSQL connection string is available from the [Supabase database connection guide](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/connecting-to-postgres).\n16. \n17. <a id=\"agents-server-env-ptbk-agents-server-database\"></a>\n18. -   `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_DATABASE`: Database backend used by Agents Server. Use `supabase` for the current hosted setup or `sqlite` for a standalone local database.\n19. \n20. <a id=\"agents-server-env-ptbk-agents-server-sqlite-path\"></a>\n21. -   `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_SQLITE_PATH`: SQLite database file used when `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_DATABASE=sqlite`. Defaults to `.promptbook/agents-server.sqlite` in the launch directory.\n22. \n23. <a id=\"agents-server-env-openai-api-key\"></a>\n24. -   `OPENAI_API_KEY`: OpenAI API key used for Agents Server chat and agent execution. Create one in the [OpenAI API key settings](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys).\n25. \n26. <a id=\"agents-server-env-postgres-url\"></a>\n27. -   `POSTGRES_URL`: PostgreSQL connection string used by Agents Server SQL access and migrations. Copy a connection string from your Supabase database connection settings.\n28. \n29. <a id=\"agents-server-env-next-public-supabase-url\"></a>\n30. -   `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`: Public Supabase project URL used by Agents Server clients. Copy the project URL from your Supabase project API settings.\n31. \n32. <a id=\"agents-server-env-next-public-supabase-anon-key\"></a>\n33. -   `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`: Public Supabase API key used by browser-side Supabase access. Copy the publishable or legacy anon key from your Supabase project API settings.\n34. \n35. <a id=\"agents-server-env-supabase-service-role-key\"></a>\n36. -   `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`: Server-only Supabase key used by trusted Agents Server routes. Copy the service-role or secret key from your Supabase project API settings and keep it private.\n37. \n38. <a id=\"agents-server-env-supabase-auto-migrate\"></a>\n39. -   `SUPABASE_AUTO_MIGRATE`: Leave this as `true` for local startup so Agents Server creates and updates the database schema automatically.\n40. \n41. <a id=\"agents-server-env-admin-password\"></a>\n42. -   `ADMIN_PASSWORD`: Password for the built-in `admin` login on a self-hosted Agents Server. Choose a private value before using the admin UI.\n43. \n44. ## Creating servers\n45. \n46. When creating new Agents server, search across the repository for [☁]\n47. \n48. -   [☁] [Add domain to environments](https://vercel.com/pavol-hejns-projects/promptbook-agents-server/settings/environments)\n49. -   [☁] Add or update the server row in the global `_Server` database table\n50. -   [☁] Run the `_Server` -> Vercel domain sync script\n51. -   [☁] [Add to `.env` file](./.env)\n52. -   [☁] Keep `SERVER_VISIBILITY=PRIVATE` by default; switch to `SERVER_VISIBILITY=PUBLIC` (or metadata `SERVER_VISIBILITY`) only when the server should be crawlable and expose sitemap entries for public agents\n53. -   [☁] Optional chat default can be set via metadata `CHAT_VISUAL_MODE` (`BUBBLE_MODE` / `ARTICLE_MODE`); users can still override in the Control panel\n54. -   [☁] If using `USE PROJECT` auto-auth, configure [GitHub App integration](./GITHUB_APP.md)\n55. -   [☁] Add the server to [the list of our servers](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1X26iMQqubsxftqD1EJNSlzPYFS94QjCFPXyKdHHDeVs/edit?gid=848307752#gid=848307752)\n56. -   [☁] Run migration script _(run new instance)_\n57. -   [☁] For testing servers, look at the server and change the `CORE_SERVER` to `https://core-test.ptbk.io/`\n58. Copy the project URL from your Supabase project API settings.\n \n <a id=\"agents-server-env-next-public-supabase-anon-key\"></a>\n -   `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`: Public Supabase API key used by browser-side Supabase access. Copy the publishable or legacy anon key from your Supabase project API settings.\n \n <a id=\"agents-server-env-supabase-service-role-key\"></a>\n -   `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`: Server-only Supabase key used by trusted Agents Server routes. Copy the service-role or secret key from your Supabase project API settings and keep it private.\n \n <a id=\"agents-server-env-supabase-auto-migrate\"></a>\n -   `SUPABASE_AUTO_MIGRATE`: Leave this as `true` for local startup so Agents Server creates and updates the database schema automatically.\n \n <a id=\"agents-server-env-admin-password\"></a>\n -   `ADMIN_PASSWORD`: Password for the built-in `admin` login on a self-hosted Agents Server. Choose a private value before using the admin UI.\n \n ## Creating servers\n \n When creating new Agents server, search across the repository for [☁]\n \n -   [☁] [Add domain to environments](https://vercel.com/pavol-hejns-projects/promptbook-agents-server/settings/environments)\n -   [☁] Add or update the server row in the global `_Server` database table\n -   [☁] Run the `_Server` -> Vercel domain sync script\n -   [☁] [Add to `.env` file](./.env)\n -   [☁] Keep `SERVER_VISIBILITY=PRIVATE` by default; switch to `SERVER_VISIBILITY=PUBLIC` (or metadata `SERVER_VISIBILITY`) only when the server should be crawlable and expose sitemap entries for public agents\n -   [☁] Optional chat default can be set via metadata `CHAT_VISUAL_MODE` (`BUBBLE_MODE` / `ARTICLE_MODE`); users can still override in the Control panel\n -   [☁] If using `USE PROJECT` auto-auth, configure [GitHub App integration](./GITHUB_APP.md)\n -   [☁] Add the server to [the list of our servers](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1X26iMQqubsxftqD1EJNSlzPYFS94QjCFPXyKdHHDeVs/edit?gid=848307752#gid=848307752)\n -   [☁] Run migration script _(run new instance)_\n -   [☁] For testing servers, look at the server and change the `CORE_SERVER` to `https://core-test.ptbk.io/`\n \n"},"toolTelemetry":{"properties":{"command":"view","options":"{\"truncateBasedOn\":\"tokenCount\",\"truncateStyle\":\"middle\"}","inputs":"[\"path\",\"view_range\",\"command\"]","resolvedPathAgainstCwd":"false","fileExtension":"[\".md\"]","viewType":"file"},"metrics":{"resultLength":4464,"resultForLlmLength":4464,"responseTokenLimit":68000},"restrictedProperties":{}}},"id":"66abb35e-c45b-494a-bc1e-dc798828803e","timestamp":"2026-05-28T18:26:37.347Z","parentId":"5b24cf74-f547-4387-9d0f-54f083d1fd5c"}

RUN_USER=\"$SUDO_USER\"\n430.     else\n431.         RUN_USER=\"$(id -un)\"\n432.     fi\n433. \n434.     RUN_GROUP=\"$(id -gn \"$RUN_USER\")\"\n435.     RUN_HOME=\"$(getent passwd \"$RUN_USER\" | cut -d: -f6)\"\n436. \n437.     if [[ -z \"$RUN_HOME\" ]]; then\n438.         fail \"Could not resolve home directory for user $RUN_USER.\"\n439.     fi\n440. }\n441. \n442. run_as_service_user() {\n443.     if [[ \"$RUN_USER\" == \"root\" ]]; then\n444.         HOME=\"$RUN_HOME\" \"$@\"\n445.         return\n446.     fi\n447. \n448.     sudo -H -u \"$RUN_USER\" env HOME=\"$RUN_HOME\" \"$@\"\n449. }\n450. \n451. check_platform() {\n452.     if [[ ! -r /etc/os-release ]]; then\n453.         fail \"This installer is intended for Ubuntu 24.04 LTS x64.\"\n454.     fi\n455. \n456.     # shellcheck source=/dev/null\n457.     source /etc/os-release\n458. \n459.     if [[ \"${ID:-}\" != \"ubuntu\" ]]; then\n460.         warn \"Detected ${PRETTY_NAME:-unknown OS}; this installer is tested on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS x64.\"\n461.     elif [[ \"${VERSION_ID:-}\" != \"24.04\" ]]; then\n462.         warn \"Detected Ubuntu ${VERSION_ID:-unknown}; this installer is tested on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS x64.\"\n463.     fi\n464. \n465.     local architecture=\"\"\n466.     architecture=\"$(dpkg --print-architecture)\"\n467.     if [[ \"$architecture\" != \"amd64\" ]]; then\n468.         fail \"Unsupported architecture $architecture. NODE_MINIMUM_VERSION=\"$minimum_version\" node -e '\n497.             const currentParts = process.versions.node.split(\".\").map(Number);\n498.             const minimumParts = (process.env.NODE_MINIMUM_VERSION || \"22.12.0\").split(\".\").map(Number);\n499.             const maxLength = Math.max(currentParts.length, minimumParts.length);\n500. \n501.             for (let index = 0; index < maxLength; index++) {\n502.                 const currentPart = currentParts[index] || 0;\n503.                 const minimumPart = minimumParts[index] || 0;\n504. \n505.                 if (currentPart > minimumPart) {\n506.                     process.exit(0);\n507.                 }\n508. \n509.                 if (currentPart < minimumPart) {\n510.                     process.exit(1);\n511.                 }\n512.             }\n513. \n514.             process.exit(0);\n515. index < maxLength; index++) {\n                 const currentPart = currentParts[index] || 0;\n                 const minimumPart = minimumParts[index] || 0;\n \n                 if (currentPart > minimumPart) {\n                     process.exit(0);\n                 }\n \n                 if (currentPart < minimumPart) {\n                     process.exit(1);\n                 }\n             }\n \n             process.exit(0);\n         ' >/dev/null 2>&1\n }\n \n resolve_node_minimum_version() {\n     if [[ -n \"$NODE_MINIMUM_VERSION\" ]]; update(values: Record<string, unknown>): LocalPostgresQueryBuilder {\n+        return new LocalPostgresQueryBuilder(this.pool, this.tableName).update(values);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Starts a delete query.\n+     */\n+    public delete(): LocalPostgresQueryBuilder {\n+        return new LocalPostgresQueryBuilder(this.pool, this.tableName).delete();\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Starts an upsert query.\n+     */\n+    public upsert(values: TODO_any, options?: LocalPostgresUpsertOptions): LocalPostgresQueryBuilder {\n+        return new LocalPostgresQueryBuilder(this.pool, this.tableName).upsert(values, options);\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Supabase-shaped thenable query builder executed by `await`.\n+ */\n+class LocalPostgresQueryBuilder implements PromiseLike<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+    private operation: LocalPostgresOperation = 'select';\n+    private selectedColumns = '*';\n+    private selectOptions: LocalPostgresSelectOptions = {};\n+    private filters: Array<LocalPostgresFilter> = [];\n+    private orFilters: Array<string> = [];\n+    private orders: Array<LocalPostgresOrder> = [];\n+    private limitCount: number | null = null;\n+    private offsetCount: number | null = null;\n+    private singleMode: 'single' | 'maybeSingle' | null = null;\n+    private mutationRows: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = [];\n+    private mutationValues: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n+    private upsertOptions: LocalPostgresUpsertOptions = {};\n+    private signal: AbortSignal | null = null;\n+    private isReturningSelection = false;\n+\n+    public constructor(\n+        private readonly pool: Pool,\n+        private readonly tableName: string,\n+    ) {}\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Configures selected columns or mutation return columns.\n+     */\n+    public select(columns = '*', options: LocalPostgresSelectOptions = {}): this {\n+        if (this.operation !== 'select') {\n+            this.isReturningSelection = true;\n+        }\n+\n+        this.selectedColumns = columns || '*';\n+        this.selectOptions = options;\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Configures inserted rows.\n+     */\n+    public insert(values: TODO_any): this {\n+        this.operation = 'insert';\n+        this.mutationRows = normalizeMutationRows(values);\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Configures updated values.\n+     */\n+    public update(values: Record<string, unknown>): this {\n+        this.operation = 'update';\n+        this.mutationValues = stripUndefinedValues(values);\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Configures row deletion.\n+     */\n+    public delete(): this {\n+        this.operation = 'delete';\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Configures inserted-or-updated rows.\n+     */\n+    public upsert(values: TODO_any, options: LocalPostgresUpsertOptions = {}): this {\n+        this.operation = 'upsert';\n+        this.mutationRows = normalizeMutationRows(values);\n+        this.upsertOptions = options;\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds equality filter.\n+     */\n+    public eq(column: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'eq', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds inequality filter.\n+     */\n+    public neq(column: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'neq', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds nullability filter.\n+     */\n+    public is(column: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'is', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds negative filter for supported operators.\n+     */\n+    public not(column: string, operator: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        if (operator === 'is') {\n+            this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'not-is', value });\n+        } else if (operator === 'eq') {\n+            this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'neq', value });\n+        }\n+\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds `IN` filter.\n+     */\n+    public in(column: string, value: ReadonlyArray<unknown>): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'in', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds less-than filter.\n+     */\n+    public lt(column: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'lt', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds less-than-or-equal filter.\n+     */\n+    public lte(column: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'lte', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds greater-than filter.\n+     */\n+    public gt(column: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'gt', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds greater-than-or-equal filter.\n+     */\n+    public gte(column: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'gte', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds SQL LIKE filter.\n+     */\n+    public like(column: string, value: string): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'like', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds case-insensitive LIKE filter.\n+     */\n+    public ilike(column: string, value: string): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'ilike', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds an OR filter in the PostgREST format used by Supabase.\n+     */\n+    public or(filter: string): this {\n+        this.orFilters.push(filter);\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds ordering.\n+     */\n+    public order(column: string, options: { ascending?: boolean; nullsFirst?: boolean } = {}): this {\n+        this.orders.push({\n+            column,\n+            ascending: options.ascending !== false,\n+            nullsFirst: options.nullsFirst,\n+        });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds a limit.\n+     */\n+    public limit(count: number): this {\n+        this.limitCount = count;\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds inclusive range pagination.\n+     */\n+    public range(from: number, to: number): this {\n+        this.offsetCount = from;\n+        this.limitCount = Math.max(0, to - from + 1);\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Marks the query as requiring exactly one row.\n+     */\n+    public single(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        this.singleMode = 'single';\n+        return this.execute();\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Marks the query as requiring at most one row.\n+     */\n+    public maybeSingle(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        this.singleMode = 'maybeSingle';\n+        return this.execute();\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Accepts an abort signal for API compatibility.\n+     */\n+    public abortSignal(signal: AbortSignal): this {\n+        this.signal = signal;\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Makes the query builder awaitable.\n+     */\n+    public then<TResult1 = LocalPostgresQueryResult, TResult2 = never>(\n+        onfulfilled?: ((value: LocalPostgresQueryResult) => TResult1 | PromiseLike<TResult1>) | null,\n+        onrejected?: ((reason: unknown) => TResult2 | PromiseLike<TResult2>) | null,\n+    ): Promise<TResult1 | TResult2> {\n+        return this.execute().then(onfulfilled, onrejected);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes the configured query.\n+     */\n+    private async execute(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        try {\n+            if (this.signal?.aborted) {\n+                throw new Error('The operation was aborted.');\n+            }\n+\n+            switch (this.operation) {\n+                case 'insert':\n+                    return this.executeInsert();\n+                case 'update':\n+                    return this.executeUpdate();\n+                case 'delete':\n+                    return this.executeDelete();\n+                case 'upsert':\n+                    return this.executeUpsert();\n+                case 'select':\n+                default:\n+                    return this.executeSelect();\n+            }\n+        } catch (error) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: normalizePostgresError(error),\n+                status: 400,\n+                statusText: 'Bad Request',\n+            };\n+        }\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes a select query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeSelect(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const selectedColumns = parseSelectedColumns(this.selectedColumns);\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const orderByClause = this.createOrderByClause();\n+        const limitClause = this.createLimitClause(queryValues);\n+        const count = this.selectOptions.count === 'exact' ? await this.executeCount(whereClause, queryValues) : null;\n+\n+        if (this.selectOptions.head) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                count,\n+                status: 200,\n+                statusText: 'OK',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [\n+                `SELECT ${createSelectExpression(selectedColumns)} FROM ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)}`,\n+                whereClause,\n+                orderByClause,\n+                limitClause,\n+            ]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>, count);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an insert query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeInsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const rows = this.mutationRows.map((row) => stripUndefinedValues(row));\n+        const columnNames = uniqueStrings(rows.flatMap((row) => Object.keys(row)));\n+\n+        const valuesSql = rows\n+            .map((row) => {\n+                const placeholders = columnNames.map((column) =>\n+                    createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(column in row ? row[column] : null)),\n+                );\n+                return `(${placeholders.join(', ')})`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ');\n+\n+        const sql = [\n+            `INSERT INTO ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an update query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeUpdate(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const updateColumns = Object.keys(this.mutationValues);\n+\n+        if (updateColumns.length === 0) {\n+            return this.createMutationResponse([]);\n+        }\n+\n+        const assignments = updateColumns.map(\n+            (column) => `${quoteIdentifier(column)} = ${createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(this.mutationValues[column]))}`,\n+        );\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [\n+                `UPDATE ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} SET ${assignments.join(', ')}`,\n+                whereClause,\n+                this.createReturningClause(),\n+            ]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes a delete query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeDelete(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [`DELETE FROM ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)}`, whereClause, this.createReturningClause()]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an upsert query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeUpsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const rows = this.mutationRows.map((row) => stripUndefinedValues(row));\n+        const columnNames = uniqueStrings(rows.flatMap((row) => Object.keys(row)));\n+        const conflictColumns = resolveUpsertConflictColumns(resolveTableBaseName(this.tableName), this.upsertOptions);\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const valuesSql = rows\n+            .map((row) => {\n+                const placeholders = columnNames.map((column) =>\n+                    createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(column in row ? row[column] : null)),\n+                );\n+                return `(${placeholders.join(', ')})`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ');\n+        const updateColumns = columnNames.filter((column) => column !== 'id');\n+        const updateAssignments = updateColumns.map(\n+            (column) => `${quoteIdentifier(column)} = EXCLUDED.${quoteIdentifier(column)}`,\n+        );\n+\n+        const onConflictClause =\n+            conflictColumns.length === 0\n+                ? ''\n+                : updateAssignments.length === 0\n+                  ? `ON CONFLICT (${conflictColumns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')}) DO NOTHING`\n+                  : `ON CONFLICT (${conflictColumns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')}) DO UPDATE SET ${updateAssignments.join(', ')}`;\n+\n+        const sql = [\n+            `INSERT INTO ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            onConflictClause,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates a mutation response, optionally loading selected mutated rows.\n+     */\n+    private createMutationResponse(rows: ReadonlyArray<Record<string, unknown>>): LocalPostgresQueryResult {\n+        if (!this.isReturningSelection) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse([...rows], null);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Applies single/maybeSingle response semantics.\n+     */\n+    private finalizeDataResponse(data: Array<Record<string, unknown>>, count: number | null): LocalPostgresQueryResult {\n+        if (this.singleMode === 'single') {\n+            if (data.length !== 1) {\n+                return {\n+                    data: null,\n+                    error: {\n+                        code: 'PGRST116',\n+                        message: `Expected exactly one row, received ${data.length}.`,\n+                    },\n+                    count,\n+                    status: 406,\n+                    statusText: 'Not Acceptable',\n+                };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0], error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.singleMode === 'maybeSingle') {\n+            if (data.length > 1) {\n+                return {\n+                    data: null,\n+                    error: {\n+                        code: 'PGRST116',\n+                        message: `Expected zero or one row, received ${data.length}.`,\n+                    },\n+                    count,\n+                    status: 406,\n+                    statusText: 'Not Acceptable',\n+                };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0] || null, error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        return { data, error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Counts rows matching the current filters.\n+     */\n+    private async executeCount(whereClause: string, whereValues: ReadonlyArray<unknown>): Promise<number> {\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [`SELECT COUNT(*)::INT AS \"count\" FROM ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)}`, whereClause].filter(Boolean).join(' '),\n+            [...whereValues],\n+        );\n+\n+        return Number(result.rows[0]?.count || 0);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL WHERE clause.\n+     */\n+    private createWhereClause(values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+        const parts: Array<string> = [];\n+\n+        for (const filter of this.filters) {\n+            parts.push(createFilterCondition(filter, values));\n+        }\n+\n+        for (const filter of this.orFilters) {\n+            const condition = createOrFilterCondition(filter, values);\n+            if (condition) {\n+                parts.push(condition);\n+            }\n+        }\n+\n+        return parts.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${parts.join(' AND ')}` : '';\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL ORDER BY clause.\n+     */\n+    private createOrderByClause(): string {\n+        if (this.orders.length === 0) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `ORDER BY ${this.orders\n+            .map((order) => {\n+                const direction = order.ascending ? 'ASC' : 'DESC';\n+                const nullOrder =\n+                    order.nullsFirst === true ? ' NULLS FIRST' : order.nullsFirst === false ? ' NULLS LAST' : '';\n+                return `${quoteIdentifier(order.column)} ${direction}${nullOrder}`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ')}`;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL LIMIT/OFFSET clause.\n+     */\n+    private createLimitClause(values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+        if (this.limitCount === null) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.offsetCount === null) {\n+            return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)}`;\n+        }\n+\n+        return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)} OFFSET ${createPlaceholder(values, this.offsetCount)}`;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL RETURNING clause for mutation queries.\n+     */\n+    private createReturningClause(): string {\n+        if (!this.isReturningSelection) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `RETURNING ${createSelectExpression(parseSelectedColumns(this.selectedColumns))}`;\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates SQL for one simple filter.\n+ */\n+function createFilterCondition(filter: LocalPostgresFilter, values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+    const column = quoteIdentifier(filter.column);\n+    const value = normalizeDatabaseValue(filter.value);\n+\n+    switch (filter.operator) {\n+        case 'eq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NULL` : `${column} = ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'neq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NOT NULL` : `${column} <> ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'is':\n+            return filter.value === null ? `${column} IS NULL` : `${column} IS NOT DISTINCT FROM ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'not-is':\n+            return filter.value === null ? `${column} IS NOT NULL` : `${column} IS DISTINCT FROM ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'in': {\n+            const normalizedValues = Array.isArray(filter.value) ? filter.value.map(normalizeDatabaseValue) : [];\n+            if (normalizedValues.length === 0) {\n+                return 'FALSE';\n+            }\n+\n+            return `${column} IN (${normalizedValues.map((normalizedValue) => createPlaceholder(values, normalizedValue)).join(', ')})`;\n+        }\n+        case 'lt':\n+            return `${column} < ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'lte':\n+            return `${column} <= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'gt':\n+            return `${column} > ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'gte':\n+            return `${column} >= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'like':\n+            return `${column} LIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE '\\\\'`;\n+        case 'ilike':\n+            return `${column} ILIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE '\\\\'`;\n+        default:\n+            return 'TRUE';\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates SQL for one PostgREST `.or(...)` filter.\n+ */\n+function createOrFilterCondition(filter: string, values: Array<unknown>): string | null {\n+    const conditions: Array<string> = [];\n+\n+    for (const part of splitPostgrestOrFilter(filter)) {\n+        const parsedFilter = parsePostgrestFilter(part);\n+        if (!parsedFilter) {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (parsedFilter.operator === 'cs') {\n+            conditions.push('FALSE');\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        conditions.push(\n+            createFilterCondition(\n+                {\n+                    column: parsedFilter.column,\n+                    operator: normalizePostgrestOperator(parsedFilter.operator),\n+                    value: decodePostgrestFilterValue(parsedFilter.value),\n+                },\n+                values,\n+            ),\n+        );\n+    }\n+\n+    if (conditions.length === 0) {\n+        return null;\n+    }\n+\n+    return `(${conditions.join(' OR ')})`;\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Splits a PostgREST OR filter while keeping JSON literals intact.\n+ */\n+function splitPostgrestOrFilter(filter: string): Array<string> {\n+    const parts: Array<string> = [];\n+    let current = '';\n+    let depth = 0;\n+    let isInsideString = false;\n+\n+    for (let index = 0; index < filter.length; index++) {\n+        const character = filter[index]!;\n+        const previousCharacter = filter[index - 1];\n+\n+        if (character === '\"' && previousCharacter !== '\\\\') {\n+            isInsideString = !isInsideString;\n+        } else if (!isInsideString && (character === '{' || character === '[')) {\n+            depth++;\n+        } else if (!isInsideString && (character === '}' || character === ']')) {\n+            depth--;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (character === ',' && depth === 0 && !isInsideString) {\n+            parts.push(current);\n+            current = '';\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        current += character;\n+    }\n+\n+    if (current) {\n+        parts.push(current);\n+    }\n+\n+    return parts.map((part) => part.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parsed PostgREST filter expression.\n+ */\n+type ParsedPostgrestFilter = {\n+    readonly column: string;\n+    readonly operator: string;\n+    readonly value: string;\n+};\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parses one PostgREST filter expression.\n+ */\n+function parsePostgrestFilter(filter: string): ParsedPostgrestFilter | null {\n+    const match = /^([^.]*)\\.([a-z]+)\\.([\\s\\S]*)$/iu.exec(filter.trim());\n+    if (!match) {\n+        return null;\n+    }\n+\n+    return {\n+        column: match[1]!,\n+        operator: match[2]!.toLowerCase(),\n+        value: match[3]!,\n+    };\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Converts PostgREST operators into internal filter operators.\n+ */\n+function normalizePostgrestOperator(operator: string): LocalPostgresFilter['operator'] {\n+    switch (operator) {\n+        case 'neq':\n+            return 'neq';\n+        case 'ilike':\n+            return 'ilike';\n+        case 'like':\n+            return 'like';\n+        case 'lt':\n+            return 'lt';\n+        case 'lte':\n+            return 'lte';\n+        case 'gt':\n+            return 'gt';\n+        case 'gte':\n+            return 'gte';\n+        case 'eq':\n+        default:\n+            return 'eq';\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Decodes a PostgREST filter value when URL-encoded by callers.\n+ */\n+function decodePostgrestFilterValue(value: string): string {\n+    try {\n+        return decodeURIComponent(value);\n+    } catch {\n+        return value;\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates a select expression from parsed columns.\n+ */\n+function createSelectExpression(columns: ReadonlyArray<string>): string {\n+    if (columns.length === 0 || columns.includes('*')) {\n+        return '*';\n+    }\n+\n+    return columns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ');\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parses a simple Supabase select column list.\n+ */\n+function parseSelectedColumns(columns: string): Array<string> {\n+    const trimmedColumns = columns.trim();\n+    if (!trimmedColumns || trimmedColumns === '*') {\n+        return ['*'];\n+    }\n+\n+    return trimmedColumns\n+        .split(',')\n+        .map((column) => column.trim())\n+        .filter(Boolean)\n+        .map((column) => column.split(':').pop() || column)\n+        .map((column) => column.replace(/\\s+/g, ''))\n+        .filter((column) => /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$/u.test(column));\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Converts mutation payloads into a uniform array of records.\n+ */\n+function normalizeMutationRows(values: TODO_any): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {\n+    if (Array.isArray(values)) {\n+        return values.map((row) => stripUndefinedValues(row || {}));\n+    }\n+\n+    return [stripUndefinedValues(values || {})];\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Removes undefined values because Supabase omits them from mutation payloads.\n+ */\n+function stripUndefinedValues(values: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> private async executeUpsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            onConflictClause,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates a mutation response, optionally loading selected mutated rows.\n+     */\n+    private createMutationResponse(rows: ReadonlyArray<Record<string,                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse([...rows], null);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Applies single/maybeSingle response    if (data.length !== 1) {\n+                return {\n+                    data: null,\n+                    error: {\n+                        code: 'PGRST116',\n+                        message:
