            };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0], error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.singleMode === 'maybeSingle') {\n+            if (data.length > 1) {\n+                return {\n+                    data: null,\n+                    error: {\n+                        code: 'PGRST116',\n+                        message: `Expected zero or one row, received ${data.length}.`,\n+                    },\n+                    count,\n+                    status: 406,\n+                    statusText: 'Not Acceptable',\n+                };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0] || null, error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        return { data, error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Counts rows matching the current filters.\n+     */\n+    private async executeCount(whereClause: string, whereValues: ReadonlyArray<unknown>):            [...whereValues],\n+        );\n+\n+        return Number(result.rows[0]?.count || 0);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL WHERE clause.\n+     */\n+    private createWhereClause(values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+        const parts: Array<string> = [];\n+\n+        for (const filter of this.filters) {\n+            parts.push(createFilterCondition(filter, values));\n+        }\n+\n+        for (const filter of this.orFilters) {\n+            const condition = createOrFilterCondition(filter, values);\n+            if (condition) {\n+                parts.push(condition);\n+            }\n+        }\n+\n+        return parts.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${parts.join(' AND ')}` : '';\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL ORDER BY clause.\n+     */\n+    private createOrderByClause(): string {\n+        if (this.orders.length === 0) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `ORDER BY ${this.orders\n+            .map((order) => {\n+                const direction = order.ascending ? 'ASC' : 'DESC';\n+                const nullOrder =\n+                    order.nullsFirst === true ? ' NULLS FIRST' : order.nullsFirst === false ? ' NULLS LAST' : '';\n+                return `${quoteIdentifier(order.column)} ${direction}${nullOrder}`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ')}`;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL LIMIT/OFFSET clause.\n+     */\n+    private createLimitClause(values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+        if (this.limitCount === null) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.offsetCount === null) {\n+            return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)}`;\n+        }\n+\n+        return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)} OFFSET ${createPlaceholder(values, this.offsetCount)}`;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL RETURNING clause for mutation queries.\n+     */\n+    private createReturningClause(): string {\n+        if (!this.isReturningSelection) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `RETURNING ${createSelectExpression(parseSelectedColumns(this.selectedColumns))}`;\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates SQL for one simple filter.\n+ */\n+function createFilterCondition(filter: LocalPostgresFilter, values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+    const column = quoteIdentifier(filter.column);\n+    const value = normalizeDatabaseValue(filter.value);\n+\n+    switch (filter.operator) {\n+        case 'eq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NULL` : `${column} = ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'neq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NOT NULL` : `${column} <> ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'is':\n+            return filter.value === null ? `${column} IS NULL` : `${column} IS NOT DISTINCT FROM ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'not-is':\n+            return filter.value === null ? `${column} IS NOT NULL` : `${column} IS DISTINCT FROM ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'in': {\n+            const normalizedValues = Array.isArray(filter.value) ? filter.value.map(normalizeDatabaseValue) : [];\n+            if (normalizedValues.length === 0) {\n+                return 'FALSE';\n+            }\n+\n+            return `${column} IN (${normalizedValues.map((normalizedValue) => createPlaceholder(values, normalizedValue)).join(', ')})`;\n+        }\n+        case 'lt':\n+            return `${column} < ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'lte':\n+            return `${column} <= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'gt':\n+            return `${column} > ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'gte':\n+            return `${column} >= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'like':\n+            return `${column} LIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE '\\\\'`;\n+        case 'ilike':\n+            return `${column} ILIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE '\\\\'`;\n+        default:\n+            return 'TRUE';\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates SQL for one PostgREST `.or(...)` filter.\n+ */\n+function createOrFilterCondition(filter: string, values: Array<unknown>): string | null {\n+    const conditions: Array<string> = [];\n+\n+    for (const part of splitPostgrestOrFilter(filter)) {\n+        const parsedFilter = parsePostgrestFilter(part);\n+        if (!parsedFilter) {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (parsedFilter.operator === 'cs') {\n+            conditions.push('FALSE');\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        conditions.push(\n+            createFilterCondition(\n+                {\n+                    column: parsedFilter.column,\n+                    operator: normalizePostgrestOperator(parsedFilter.operator),\n+                    value: decodePostgrestFilterValue(parsedFilter.value),\n+                },\n+                values,\n+            ),\n+        );\n+    }\n+\n+    if (conditions.length === 0) {\n+        return null;\n+    }\n+\n+    return `(${conditions.join(' OR ')})`;\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Splits a PostgREST OR filter while keeping JSON literals intact.\n+ */\n+function splitPostgrestOrFilter(filter: string): Array<string> {\n+    const parts: Array<string> = [];\n+    let current = '';\n+    let depth = 0;\n+    let isInsideString = false;\n+\n+    for (let index = 0; index < filter.length; index++) {\n+        const character = filter[index]!;\n+        const previousCharacter = filter[index - 1];\n+\n+        if (character === '\"' && previousCharacter !== '\\\\') {\n+            isInsideString = !isInsideString;\n+        } else if (!isInsideString && (character === '{' || character === '[')) {\n+            depth++;\n+        } else if (!isInsideString && (character === '}' || character === ']')) {\n+            depth--;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (character === ',' && depth === 0 && !isInsideString) {\n+            parts.push(current);\n+            current = '';\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        current += character;\n+    }\n+\n+    if (current) {\n+        parts.push(current);\n+    }\n+\n+    return parts.map((part) => part.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parsed PostgREST filter expression.\n+ */\n+type ParsedPostgrestFilter = {\n+    readonly column: string;\n+    readonly operator: string;\n+    readonly value: string;\n+};\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parses one PostgREST filter expression.\n+ */\n+function parsePostgrestFilter(filter: string): ParsedPostgrestFilter | null {\n+    const match = /^([^.]*)\\.([a-z]+)\\.([\\s\\S]*)$/iu.exec(filter.trim());\n+    if (!match) {\n+        return null;\n+    }\n+\n+    return {\n+        column: match[1]!,\n+        operator: match[2]!.toLowerCase(),\n+        value: match[3]!,\n+    };\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Converts PostgREST operators into internal filter operators.\n+ */\n+function normalizePostgrestOperator(operator: string): LocalPostgresFilter['operator'] {\n+    switch (operator) {\n+        case 'neq':\n+            return 'neq';\n+        case 'ilike':\n+            return 'ilike';\n+        case 'like':\n+            return 'like';\n+        case 'lt':\n+            return 'lt';\n+        case 'lte':\n+            return 'lte';\n+        case 'gt':\n+            return 'gt';\n+        case 'gte':\n+            return 'gte';\n+        case 'eq':\n+        default:\n+            return 'eq';\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Decodes a PostgREST filter value when URL-encoded by callers.\n+ */\n+function decodePostgrestFilterValue(value: string): string {\n+    try {\n+        return decodeURIComponent(value);\n+    } catch {\n+        return value;\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates a select expression from parsed columns.\n+ */\n+function createSelectExpression(columns: ReadonlyArray<string>): string {\n+    if (columns.length === 0 || columns.includes('*')) {\n+        return '*';\n+    }\n+\n+    return columns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ');\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parses a simple Supabase select column list.\n+ */\n+function parseSelectedColumns(columns: string): Array<string> {\n+    const trimmedColumns = columns.trim();\n+    if (!trimmedColumns || trimmedColumns === '*') {\n+        return ['*'];\n+    }\n+\n+    return trimmedColumns\n+        .split(',')\n+        .map((column) => column.trim())\n+        .filter(Boolean)\n+        .map((column) => column.split(':').pop() || column)\n+        .map((column) => column.replace(/\\s+/g, ''))\n+        .filter((column) => /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$/u.test(column));\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Converts mutation payloads into a uniform array of records.\n+ */\n+function normalizeMutationRows(values: TODO_any): {})];\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Removes undefined values because Supabase omits them from mutation payloads.\n+ */\n+function stripUndefinedValues(values: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown>    }\n+\n+    return result;\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Resolves a table base name from the actual prefixed table name.\n+ */\n+function resolveTableBaseName(tableName: string): string {\n+    const next positional placeholder.\n+ */\n+function createPlaceholder(values: Array<unknown>, value: unknown): string {\n+    values.push(value);\n+    return `$${values.length}`;\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Quotes then<TResult1 = LocalPostgresQueryResult, TResult2 = never>(\n+        onfulfilled?: ((value: LocalPostgresQueryResult) => TResult1 | PromiseLike<TResult1>) | null,\n+        onrejected?: ((reason: configured query.\n+     */\n+    private async execute(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        try {\n+            if (this.signal?.aborted) {\n+                throw new Error('The operation was aborted.');\n+            }\n+\n+            switch (this.operation) {\n+                case 'insert':\n+                    return this.executeInsert();\n+                case 'update':\n+                    return this.executeUpdate();\n+                case 'delete':\n+                    return this.executeDelete();\n+                case 'upsert':\n+                    return this.executeUpsert();\n+                case 'select':\n+                default:\n+                    return this.executeSelect();\n+            }\n+        } catch (error) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: normalizePostgresError(error),\n+                status: 400,\n+                statusText: 'Bad Request',\n+            };\n+        }\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes a select query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeSelect(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const selectedColumns = parseSelectedColumns(this.selectedColumns);\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const orderByClause = this.createOrderByClause();\n+        const limitClause = this.createLimitClause(queryValues);\n+        const count = this.selectOptions.count === 'exact' ? await this.executeCount(whereClause, queryValues) : null;\n+\n+        if (this.selectOptions.head) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                count,\n+                status: 200,\n+                statusText: 'OK',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [\n+                `SELECT ${createSelectExpression(selectedColumns)} FROM ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)}`,\n+                whereClause,\n+                orderByClause,\n+                limitClause,\n+            ]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>, count);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an insert query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeInsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const Object.keys(row)));\n+\n+        const valuesSql = rows\n+            .map((row) => {\n+                const placeholders = columnNames.map((column) =>\n+                    createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(column in row ? row[column] : null)),\n+                );\n+                return `(${placeholders.join(', ')})`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ');\n+\n+        const sql = [\n+            `INSERT INTO ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an update query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeUpdate(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const updateColumns = Object.keys(this.mutationValues);\n+\n+        if (updateColumns.length === 0) {\n+            return this.createMutationResponse([]);\n+        }\n+\n+        const assignments = updateColumns.map(\n+            (column) => `${quoteIdentifier(column)} = ${createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(this.mutationValues[column]))}`,\n+        );\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [\n+                `UPDATE ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} SET ${assignments.join(', ')}`,\n+                whereClause,\n+                this.createReturningClause(),\n+            ]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const result = await private async executeUpsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const rows = this.mutationRows.map((row) => stripUndefinedValues(row));\n+ this.upsertOptions);\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const valuesSql = rows\n+            .map((row) => {\n+                const placeholders = columnNames.map((column) =>\n+                    createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(column in row ? row[column] : null)),\n+                );\n+                return `(${placeholders.join(', ')})`;\n+ ''\n+                : updateAssignments.length === 0\n+                  ? `ON CONFLICT (${conflictColumns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')}) DO NOTHING`\n+                  : `ON CONFLICT (${conflictColumns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')}) DO UPDATE SET ${updateAssignments.join(', ')}`;\n+\n+        const sql = [\n+            `INSERT INTO ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            onConflictClause,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string,                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse([...rows], null);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Applies single/maybeSingle response    if (data.length !== 1) {\n+                return {\n+                    data: null,\n+                    error: {\n+                        code: 'PGRST116',\n+                        message:             };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0], error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.singleMode === 'maybeSingle') {\n+            if (data.length > 1) {\n+                return {\n+                    data: null,\n+                    error: {\n+                        code: 'PGRST116',\n+                        message:             };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0] || null, error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        return { data, error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Counts rows matching the current filters.\n+     */\n+    private async executeCount(whereClause: string, whereValues: ReadonlyArray<unknown>):            [...whereValues],\n+        );\n+\n+        return Number(result.rows[0]?.count || 0);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL WHERE clause.\n+     */\n+    private parts.push(condition);\n+            }\n+        }\n+\n+        return parts.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${parts.join(' AND ')}` : '';\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL ORDER BY clause.\n+     */\n+    private createOrderByClause(): string {\n+        if (this.orders.length === 0) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `ORDER BY ${this.orders\n+            .map((order) => {\n+                const direction = order.ascending ? 'ASC' : 'DESC';\n+                const nullOrder =\n+                    order.nullsFirst === true ? ' NULLS FIRST' : order.nullsFirst === false ? ' NULLS LAST' : '';\n+                return `${quoteIdentifier(order.column)} ${direction}${nullOrder}`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ')}`;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL LIMIT/OFFSET clause.\n+     */\n+    private createLimitClause(values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+        if (this.limitCount === null) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.offsetCount === null) {\n+            return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)}`;\n+        }\n+\n+        return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)} OFFSET ${createPlaceholder(values, this.offsetCount)}`;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL RETURNING clause for mutation queries.\n+     */\n+    private createReturningClause(): string {\n+ Creates SQL for one simple filter.\n+ */\n+function createFilterCondition(filter: LocalPostgresFilter, values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+    const column = quoteIdentifier(filter.column);\n+    const value = normalizeDatabaseValue(filter.value);\n+\n+    switch (filter.operator) {\n+        case 'eq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NULL` : `${column} = ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'neq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NOT NULL` : `${column} <> ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'is':\n+ (${normalizedValues.map((normalizedValue) => createPlaceholder(values, normalizedValue)).join(', ')})`;\n+        }\n+        case 'lt':\n+            return `${column} < ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'lte':\n+            return `${column} <= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'gt':\n+            return `${column} > ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'gte':\n+            return `${column} >= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'like':\n+            return `${column} LIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE '\\\\'`;\n+        case 'ilike':\n+            return `${column} ILIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE '\\\\'`;\n+        default:\n+            return 'TRUE';\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates SQL for one PostgREST `.or(...)` filter.\n+ */\n+function createOrFilterCondition(filter: string, values: Array<unknown>): string | null {\n+    const conditions: Array<string> = [];\n+\n+    for (const part of splitPostgrestOrFilter(filter)) {\n+        const parsedFilter = parsePostgrestFilter(part);\n+        if (!parsedFilter) {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (parsedFilter.operator === 'cs') {\n+            conditions.push('FALSE');\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        conditions.push(\n+            createFilterCondition(\n+                {\n+ Array<string> {\n+    const parts: Array<string> = [];\n+    let current = '';\n+    let depth = 0;\n+    let isInsideString = false;\n+\n+    for (let index = 0; index < filter.length; index++) {\n+        const character = filter[index]!;\n+        const previousCharacter = filter[index - 1];\n+\n+        if (character === '\"' && previousCharacter !== '\\\\') {\n+            isInsideString = ']')) {\n+            depth--;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (character === ',' && depth === 0 && !isInsideString) {\n+            parts.push(current);\n+            current = '';\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        current += character;\n+    }\n+\n+    if (current) {\n+        parts.push(current);\n+    }\n+\n+    return parts.map((part) => readonly value: string;\n+};\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parses one PostgREST filter expression.\n+ */\n+function parsePostgrestFilter(filter: string): ParsedPostgrestFilter | null {\n+    const match = The agent source is parsed and processed by the Promptbook Engine to create the actual AI agent that can interact with users and perform tasks.\nThis agent source is internally converted to a structured format called \"agent model requirements\" which are the actual raw technical instructions for the AI model to run the agent.\n\nThere is a standalone book language documentation on each agent server on `/api/docs/book.md` route, for example `https://pavol-hejny.ptbk.io/api/docs/book.md`.\nUse it as a reference for the syntax and semantics of the book language, and modify `apps/agents-server/src/utils/bookLanguageDocumentation/createStandaloneBookLanguageMarkdown.ts` if it is relevant to the change you are doing.\n\n#### Commitments _(as additional context and part of Book Language)_\n\nCommitments are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents written in `book` language.\n\n-   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, `CLOSED`, etc.\n-   They are in the folder `src/commitments`.\n-   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g. `PERSONA`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, etc. on a beginning of the line and ends by a new commitment or the end of the book.\n-   There is a general pattern that the commitment keyword is followed by a space and then by the content of the commitment, for example:\n    -   `PERSONA You are a helpful assistant that helps with cooking recipes.`\n    -   `USE SEARCH ENGINE Search only in French.`\n-   In the commitment context, you can reference external agents, for example:\n    -   `TEAM You can talk to {Criminal lawyer} and {Financial advisor}`\n\n## Coding rules\n\n-   Keep in mind the DRY _(don't repeat yourself)_ principle.\n-   Keep in mind the SOLID principles.\n-   Do a proper analysis of the current functionality before you start implementing.\n-   Keep small responsibilities of functions and classes, avoid creating big functions or classes that do many things.\n-   When throwing errors, throw [branded errors](src/errors) and use `spaceTrim` utility to write clear and well-formatted multiline detailed error messages.\n    - The commitments there are hardcoded.\n        -   `createAgentModelRequirements` which is an asynchronous function that creates model requirements it applies each commitment one by one and works asynchronously.\n\n## Common rules\n\n-   Always analyze the context and requirements before generating any code.\n-   Write clear, maintainable, and well-documented code.\n-   Write JSDoc comments for all entities - functions, classes, types, top-level constants, etc.\n    -   When this entity is exported from the file and it is under `src` folder _(not for example in the `apps` folder)_, it must be marked either as `@public` or `@private` at the end of the JSDoc comment.\n    -   For example: \"@private internal utility of <Chat/>\" / \"@public exported from `@promptbook/browser`\"\n    -   If you don't know, prefer to mark it as private, we can always change it to public later, but changing from public to private may cause breaking changes.\n-   You (the AI coding agent) are running inside a Node process, so do not kill all Node processes such as `taskkill /F /IM node.exe`. If you need to stop something you spawned, kill only that specific process, for example by PID or by port.\n\n## Additional context\n\n-   Attached images (if any) are relative to the root of the project.\n\n### The Agents Server menu _(as additional context)_\n\nThe menu of the agent server looks like this:\n\n1. The navigation hierarchy\n    - Icon and Server name _(for example Promptbook Agents Server)_\n    - arrow \">\" and Agents or picked agent name (organized in folders)\n    - arrow \">\" and the view Profile / Chat / Book of the agent or nothing if no agent is picked\n2. The menu items\n    - Documentation\n    - System\n3. Control panel and user menu\n    - Control panel\n    - User menu with the avatar and the name of the user\n\n### Database migrations for Agents server _(as additional context)_\n\n-   Migrations are located in `apps/agents-server/src/database/migrations`\n-   Be aware that table names in migrations have prefix `prefix_` _(look at existing migrations for reference)_\n-   Migrations should be backwards compatible:\n    -   Meaning that earlier versions of the server should be able to run with the database after migration without any issues.\n    -   This is important because we want to have the same database for production and preview environments.\n    -   This database will be migrated to the latest version of the preview environment, but the production environment should be able to run with it without any issues.\n    -   The only thing that can happen is that older versions of the server will not be able to use new features.\n    -   Adding new columns, tables, etc. is fine, but do not remove or rename existing ones, and do not change the meaning of existing columns or tables.\n    -   When in doubt, prefer to add new things instead of changing existing ones.\n\n### Metadata of Agents server _(as additional context)_\n\n-   There is a table called `Metadata`.\n-   It has `key` and `value` fields.\n-   It is a similar concept to configuration, but this configuration can be changed by administrators in the Agents Server website.\n\n### Book Language blueprint _(as additional context)_\n\nBook language is a domain-specific language used for defining AI agents in the Promptbook Engine and Agents server.\nIt has lightweight syntax and keywords (the commitments) that allow you to define the \"soul\" of the agent.\nThe book language is designed to be human-readable and easy to write, while also being powerful enough to express complex agent behaviors.\n\nEvery agent has its source defined in the book language, which is called \"agent source\". The agent source is parsed and processed by the Promptbook Engine to create the actual AI agent that can interact with users and perform tasks.\nThis agent source is internally converted to a structured format called \"agent model requirements\" which are the actual raw technical instructions for the AI model to run the agent.\n\nThere is a standalone book language documentation on each agent server on `/api/docs/book.md` route, for example `https://pavol-hejny.ptbk.io/api/docs/book.md`.\nUse it as a reference for the syntax and semantics of the book language, and modify `apps/agents-server/src/utils/bookLanguageDocumentation/createStandaloneBookLanguageMarkdown.ts` if it is relevant to the change you are doing.\n\n#### Commitments _(as additional context and part of Book Language)_\n\nCommitments are basic syntax elements that add specific functionalities to AI agents written in `book` language.\n\n-   They are used in `agentSource`, there are commitments like `PERSONA`, `RULE`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE BROWSER`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, `META IMAGE`, `CLOSED`, etc.\n-   They are in the folder `src/commitments`.\n-   Each commitment starts with a keyword, e.g. `PERSONA`, `KNOWLEDGE`, `USE SEARCH ENGINE`, etc. on a beginning of the line and ends by a new commitment or the end of the book.\n-   There is a general pattern that the commitment keyword is followed by a space and then by the content of the commitment, for example:\n    -   `PERSONA You are a helpful assistant that helps with cooking recipes.`\n    -   `USE SEARCH ENGINE Search only in French.`\n-   In the commitment context, you can reference external agents, for example:\n    -   `TEAM You can talk to {Criminal lawyer} and {Financial advisor}`\n\n## Coding rules\n\n-   Keep in mind the DRY _(don't repeat yourself)_ principle.\n-   Keep in mind the SOLID principles.\n-   Do a proper analysis of the current functionality before you start implementing.\n-   Keep small responsibilities of functions and classes, avoid creating big functions or classes that do many things.\n-   When throwing errors, throw [branded errors](src/errors) and use `spaceTrim` utility to write clear and well-formatted multiline detailed error messages.\n    -   Format errors as markdown, for example `variables` should be in backticks and important notes can be in bold.\n-   Constants should always be `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`.\n-   Boolean variables should always be prefixed with `is`, for example `isUserChatJobLeaseExpired` or `IS_DEBUG_MODE`.\n-   Do not use abbreviations, for example use `isExpired` instead of `isExp`, `translateMessage` instead of `t`, etc.\n    -   It is fine to use well-known abbreviations, for example `id`, `url`, `html`, etc.\n-   When writing multiline strings, use `spaceTrim` utility.\n-   Do only the change described in the prompt. Do not add any additional features or make any additional changes that are not described there.\n    -   If you find some critical issue that is not described in the prompt, report it to `./AGENT_REPORT.md` in the root of the project.\n\n\n\n<system_reminder>\n<sql_tables>No tables currently exist. AgentsServerSqliteDatabase,\n8. } from './sqlite/$provideAgentsServerSqliteDatabase';\n9. \n10. /**\n11.  * Query shape sent by Embedded Prisma Studio adapters.\n12.  *\n13.  * @private internal utility of Agents Server database admin\n14.  */\n15. export type DatabaseAdminQuery = {\n16.     readonly sql: string;\n17.     readonly parameters?: ReadonlyArray<unknown>;\n18.     readonly transformations?: Partial<Record<string, 'json-parse'>>;\n19. };\n20. \n21. /**\n22.  * SQL executor surface required by the Embedded Prisma Studio BFF endpoint.\n23.  *\n24.  * @private internal utility of Agents Server database admin\n25.  */\n26. export type DatabaseAdminExecutor = {\n27.     readonly execute: (query: DatabaseAdminQuery) => Promise<Array<Record<string, unknown>>>;\n28.     readonly executeTransaction: (\n29.         queries: ReadonlyArray<DatabaseAdminQuery>,\n30.     ) => Promise<Array<Array<Record<string, unknown>>>>;\n31.     readonly lintSql: (details: StudioBFFSqlLintDetails) => Promise<StudioBFFSqlLintResult>;\n32. };\n33. \n34. /**\n35.  * Shared PostgreSQL pool for Embedded Prisma Studio requests.\n36.  */\n37. let databaseAdminPostgresPool: mode.\n43.  *\n44.  * @private exported from Agents Server database utilities\n45.  */\n46. export function $provideDatabaseAdminExecutor(): DatabaseAdminExecutor {\n47.     return isAgentsServerSqliteMode() ? $provideSqliteDatabaseAdminExecutor() : $providePostgresDatabaseAdminExecutor();\n48. }\n49. \n50. /**\n51.  * Provides the PostgreSQL raw SQL executor used by Embedded Prisma Studio.\n52.  *\n53.  * @returns PostgreSQL-backed database admin executor.\n54.  */\n55. function $providePostgresDatabaseAdminExecutor(): DatabaseAdminExecutor {\n56.     const pool = $provideDatabaseAdminPostgresPool();\n57. \n58.     return {\n59.         execute: (query) => executePostgresDatabaseAdminQuery(pool, query),\n60.         executeTransaction: (queries) => executePostgresDatabaseAdminTransaction(pool, queries),\n61.         lintSql: (details) => Promise.resolve({ diagnostics: [], schemaVersion: details.schemaVersion }),\n62.     };\n63. }\n64. \n65. /**\n66.  * Provides the SQLite raw SQL executor used by Embedded Prisma Studio.\n67.  *\n68.  * @returns SQLite-backed database admin executor.\n69.  */\n70. function $provideSqliteDatabaseAdminExecutor(): DatabaseAdminExecutor {\n71.     const database = $provideAgentsServerSqliteDatabase();\n72. \n73.     return {\n74.         execute: (query) => Promise.resolve(executeSqliteDatabaseAdminQuery(database, query)),\n75.         executeTransaction: (queries) =>\n76.             Promise.resolve(\n77.                 database.transaction((transactionQueries: ReadonlyArray<DatabaseAdminQuery>) =>\n78.                     transactionQueries.map((query) => executeSqliteDatabaseAdminQuery(database, query)),\n79.                 )(queries),\n80.             ),\n81.         lintSql: (details) => Promise.resolve({ diagnostics: [], schemaVersion: details.schemaVersion }),\n82.     };\n83. }\n84. \n85. /**\n86.  * Provides the shared PostgreSQL connection pool for raw database admin access.\n87.  *\n88.  * @returns Shared PostgreSQL pool.\n89.  */\n90. function $provideDatabaseAdminPostgresPool(): Pool {\n91.     if (!databaseAdminPostgresPool) {\n92.         databaseAdminPostgresPool = new Pool({\n93.             connectionString: resolvePostgresConnectionString(),\n94.             ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false },\n95.         });\n96.     }\n97. \n98.     return databaseAdminPostgresPool;\n99. }\n100. \n101. /**\n102.  * Executes one PostgreSQL query for Embedded Prisma Studio.\n103.  *\n104.  * @param client - PostgreSQL pool or transaction client.\n105.  * @param query - SQL query generated by Embedded Prisma Studio.\n106.  * @returns JSON-safe result rows.\n107.  */\n108. async function executePostgresDatabaseAdminQuery(\n109.     client: Pick<Pool | PoolClient, 'query'>,\n110.     query: DatabaseAdminQuery,\n111. ): Promise<Array<Record<string, unknown>>> {\n112.     const result = await client.query(query.sql, normalizeDatabaseAdminParameters(query.parameters));\n113.     return normalizeDatabaseAdminRows(query, result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n114. }\n115. \n116. /**\n117.  * Executes PostgreSQL queries inside one database transaction.\n118.  *\n119.  * @param pool - Shared PostgreSQL pool.\n120.  * @param queries - SQL queries generated by Embedded Prisma Studio.\n121.  * @returns Ordered result sets for each query.\n122.  */\n123. async function executePostgresDatabaseAdminTransaction(\n124.     pool: Pool,\n125.     queries: ReadonlyArray<DatabaseAdminQuery>,\n126. ): Promise<Array<Array<Record<string, unknown>>>> {\n127.     const client = await pool.connect();\n128. \n129.     try {\n130.         await client.query('BEGIN');\n131.         const results: Array<Array<Record<string, unknown>>> = [];\n132. \n133.         for (const query of queries) {\n134.             results.push(await executePostgresDatabaseAdminQuery(client, query));\n135.         }\n136. \n137.         await client.query('COMMIT');\n138.         return results;\n139.     } catch (error) {\n140.         await client.query('ROLLBACK').catch(() => undefined);\n141.         throw error;\n142.     } finally {\n143.         client.release();\n144.     }\n145. }\n146. \n147. /**\n148.  * Executes one SQLite query for Embedded Prisma Studio.\n149.  *\n150.  * @param database - Shared SQLite database.\n151.  * @param query - SQL query generated by Embedded Prisma Studio.\n152.  * @returns JSON-safe result rows.\n153.  */\n154. function executeSqliteDatabaseAdminQuery(\n155.     database: AgentsServerSqliteDatabase,\n156.     query: DatabaseAdminQuery,\n157. ): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {\n158.     const statement = database.prepare(query.sql);\n159.     const parameters = normalizeDatabaseAdminParameters(query.parameters);\n160.     const isReader = statement.reader === true;\n161.     const rows = isReader ? statement.all(...parameters) : [];\n162. \n163.     if (!isReader) {\n164.         statement.run(...parameters);\n165.     }\n166. \n167.     return normalizeDatabaseAdminRows(query, rows);\n168. }\n169. \n170. /**\n171.  * Normalizes query parameters before passing them into SQL drivers.\n172.  *\n173.  * @param parameters - Promise<void> {\n172.     const runtimePaths = await resolveAgentsServerRuntimePaths();\n173.     loadAgentsServerProjectEnvironment(runtimePaths.launchWorkingDirectory);\n174.     await Promise.all([\n175.         mkdir(runtimePaths.agentRootPath, { recursive: true }),\n176.         mkdir(runtimePaths.logDirectoryPath, { recursive: true }),\n177.     ]);\n178. \n179.     const createAgentsServerChildEnvironment(options.port, runtimePaths.agentRootPath);\n182.     let nextServerProcess: ChildProcess | undefined;\n183.     let stopUserChatJobWorkerPump: (() => void) | ${runtimePaths.agentRootPath}.`);\n187.     logRunnerEvent(logStreams.runner, `Agents Server URL: http://localhost:${options.port}.`);\n188. \n189.     const processExitHandler = (): void => {\n190.         stopChildProcess(nextServerProcess);\n191.     };\n192.     process.once('exit', processExitHandler);\n193. \n194.     try {\n195.         const buildArtifacts = await ensureAgentsServerBuild({\n196.             appPath: runtimePaths.appPath,\n197.             environment: childEnvironment,\n198.             isBuildForced: options.isBuildForced,\n199. 'next-build',\n203.                     logStream: logStreams.next,\n204.                     state,\n205.                 });\n206.             },\n207.             onBuildOutput: (chunk) => {\n208.                 forwardChildOutput(chunk, {\n209.                     label: 'next-build',\n210.                     logStream: logStreams.next,\n211.                     state,\n212.                 });\n213.             },\n214.         });\n215.         const runtimeChildEnvironment = createAgentsServerRuntimeEnvironment(\n216.             childEnvironment,\n217.             buildArtifacts.nodeModulesPath,\n218.         ) as AgentsServerChildEnvironment;\n219.         const builtRuntimePaths: AgentsServerRuntimePaths = {\n220.             ...runtimePaths,\n221.        state,\n247.                         `Agents Server running at http://localhost:${options.port}. Next server and wires its logs into the foreground dashboard.\n292.  */\n293. function startNextServer(options: {\n294.     readonly nextCliPath: string;\n295.     readonly options: StartAgentsServerOptions;\n296.     readonly runtimePaths: AgentsServerRuntimePaths;\n297.     readonly childEnvironment: NodeJS.ProcessEnv;\n298.     readonly logStreams: options.runtimePaths.appPath,\n312.         env: options.childEnvironment,\n313.         stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],\n314.     });\n315. \n316.     bindChildOutput(commandProcess, {\n317. options.state.isContinuing = false;\n333.         logRunnerEvent(options.logStreams.runner, `Agents Server Next process failed: ${error.message}`);\n334.         addUiError(options.state, readonly state: AgentsServerSupervisorState;\n349.     },\n350. ): void {\n351.     commandProcess.stdout?.on('data', (chunk) => {\n352.         forwardChildOutput(chunk.toString(), options);\n353.          process.stdout.write(`${labeledLine}\\n`);\n383.         }\n384.     }\n385. }\n386. \n387. /**\n388.  * Creates the subprocess environment for Next and its internal local runner process.cwd();\n392. \n393.     return {\n394.         ...process.env,\n395.         PORT: String(port),\n396.         NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL || `http://localhost:${port}`,\n397.         [PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_AGENT_ROOT_ENV]: agentRootPath,\n398. PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_USER_CHAT_WORKER_TOKEN:\n404.             process.env.PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_USER_CHAT_WORKER_TOKEN ||\n405.             randomBytes(LOCAL_USER_CHAT_WORKER_TOKEN_BYTE_LENGTH).toString('hex'),\n406.     };\n407. }\n408. \n409. /**\n410.  * Creates local no-git agent runner options for folders managed by the Agents Server database.\n411.  */\n412. function createLocalAgentRunOptions(options: StartAgentsServerOptions): AgentRunOptions {\n413.     return {\n414.         agentName: options.agentName,\n415.         model: options.model,\n416.         noUi: options.noUi,\n417.         thinkingLevel: options.thinkingLevel,\n418.         noCommit: true,\n419.         ignoreGitChanges: true,\n420.         normalizeLineEndings: false,\n421.         allowCredits: options.allowCredits,\n422.         autoPush: false,\n423.         autoPull: false,\n424.         autoClone: false,\n425.     };\n426. }\n427. \n428. /**\n429.  * Starts periodic internal worker calls that queue and reconcile local message-folder jobs.\n430.  */\n431. function startUserChatJobWorkerPump(options: {\n432.     readonly port: number_port;\n433.     readonly environment: AgentsServerChildEnvironment;\n434.     readonly logStreams: AgentsServerLogStreams;\n435.     readonly state: AgentsServerSupervisorState;\n436. }): () => void {\n437.     let isTickRunning = false;\n438.     const interval = setInterval(() => {\n439.         if (!options.state.isContinuing || isTickRunning) {\n440.             return;\n441.         }\n442. \n443.         isTickRunning = true;\n444.         triggerUserChatJobWorkerTick(options)\n445.             .then(() => {\n446.                 clearUserChatJobWorkerError(options.state);\n447.             })\n448.             .catch((error) => {\n449.                 const message = error instanceof Error ? 'Environment variable `POSTGRES_URL` or `DATABASE_URL` must be defined.';\n32. \n33. /**\n34.  * Lists all user chats for one agent ordered by last activity.\n35.  */\n36. export async function listUserChats(options: ListUserChatsOptions): Promise<Array<UserChatRecord>> {\n37.     const { userId, viewerIsAdmin, agentPermanentId, includeExternalChats = false } = options;\n38.     const userChatTable = await provideUserChatTable();\n39.     const shouldLoadExternalChats = viewerIsAdmin && includeExternalChats;\n40.     const query = shouldLoadExternalChats\n41.         ? AND (\"source\" <> '${USER_CHAT_SOURCES.WEB_UI}' OR \"userId\" = $2)\n84.         `\n85.         : `\n86.             \"userId\" = $1\n87.             AND \"agentPermanentId\" = $2\n88. SELECT\n97.                     \"id\",\n98.                     \"createdAt\",\n99.                     \"updatedAt\",\n100.                     \"lastMessageAt\",\n101.                     \"title\",\n102.                     \"source\",\n103. WHERE UPPER(COALESCE(message.\"value\"->>'sender', '')) = 'USER'\n109.                             ORDER BY message.\"ordinality\" ASC\n110.                             LIMIT 1\n111.                         ),\n112.                         ''\n113.                     ) AS \"firstUserMessageContent\",\n114.                     COALESCE(\n115. FROM ${userChatTableName}\n138.                 WHERE ${whereClause}\n139. messagesCount: parseNonNegativeInteger(row.messagesCount),\n186.         firstUserMessageContent: row.firstUserMessageContent || '',\n187.         lastPreviewMessageContent: row.lastPreviewMessageContent || '',\n188.         pendingAssistantMessageCount: parseNonNegativeInteger(row.pendingAssistantMessageCount),\n189.     };\n190. }\n191. \n192. /**\n193.  * Parses one Agents Server is the main web application where Promptbook agents live\n4. \n5. ## Local CLI configuration\n6. \n7. Use `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_DATABASE=supabase` for a Supabase-backed server or `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_DATABASE=sqlite` for a standalone local database in `.promptbook`. When using Supabase, fill the initialized values from your project before starting the server. The Supabase project URL and API keys are available in the [Supabase project API settings](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/api/api-keys), and the PostgreSQL connection string is available from the [Supabase database connection guide](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/connecting-to-postgres).\n16. \n17. <a id=\"agents-server-env-ptbk-agents-server-database\"></a>\n18. -   `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_DATABASE`: Database backend used by Agents Server. Use `supabase` for the current hosted setup or `sqlite` for a standalone local database.\n19. \n20. <a id=\"agents-server-env-ptbk-agents-server-sqlite-path\"></a>\n21. -   `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_SQLITE_PATH`: SQLite database file used when `PTBK_AGENTS_SERVER_DATABASE=sqlite`. Defaults to `.promptbook/agents-server.sqlite` in the launch directory.\n22. \n23. <a id=\"agents-server-env-openai-api-key\"></a>\n24. -   `OPENAI_API_KEY`: OpenAI API key used for Agents Server chat and agent execution. then\n361.         log \"Resources OK: $(format_mib \"$total_memory_mib\") memory and $(format_mib \"$available_disk_space_mib\") free disk.\"\n362.         return\n363.     fi\n364. \n365.     additional_swap_mib=$((MINIMUM_REQUIRED_MEMORY_MIB - total_memory_mib))\n366.     warn \"Only $(format_mib \"$total_memory_mib\") total memory (RAM + swap) is available. Agents Server requires at least $(format_mib \"$MINIMUM_REQUIRED_MEMORY_MIB\").\"\n367. \n368.     if ! prompt_yes_no \"Add $(format_mib \"$additional_swap_mib\") of swap at $PROMPTBOOK_SWAP_FILE?\" \"yes\"; then\n369.         fail \"Installation stopped because the VPS does not have enough memory.\"\n370.     fi\n371. \n372.     confirm_disk_space_after_swap \"$install_disk_check_path\" \"$available_disk_space_mib\" \"$additional_swap_mib\"\n373.     add_required_swap_file \"$additional_swap_mib\"\n374.     total_memory_mib=\"$(get_total_memory_mib)\"\n375. \n376.     if [[ \"$total_memory_mib\" -lt \"$MINIMUM_REQUIRED_MEMORY_MIB\" ]]; then\n377.         fail \"Swap was configured, but total memory is still only $(format_mib \"$total_memory_mib\").\"\n378.     fi\n379. \n380.     log \"Swap configured. Total memory is now $(format_mib \"$total_memory_mib\").\"\n381. }\n382. \n383. append_domain() {\n384.     local raw_domain=\"$1\"\n385.     local normalized_domain=\"\"\n386.     local existing_domain=\"\"\n387. \n388.     normalized_domain=\"$(normalize_domain \"$raw_domain\")\"\n389.     if [[ -z \"$normalized_domain\" ]]; then\n390.         fail \"Invalid domain '$raw_domain'. Use one or more DNS hostnames, for example example.com,www.example.com.\"\n391.     fi\n392. \n393.     for existing_domain in \"${DOMAINS[@]}\"; do\n394.         if [[ \"$existing_domain\" == \"$normalized_domain\" ]]; then\n395.             return\n396.         fi\n397.     done\n398. \n399.     DOMAINS+=(\"$normalized_domain\")\n400. }\n401. \n402. set_domains_from_csv() {\n403.     local raw_domains=\"$1\"\n404.     local domain=\"\"\n405. \n406.     DOMAINS=()\n407.     IFS=',' read -ra requested_domains <<< \"$raw_domains\"\n408.     for domain in \"${requested_domains[@]}\"; do\n409.         if [[ -z \"${domain//[[:space:]]/}\" ]]; then\n410.             continue\n411.         fi\n412.         append_domain \"$domain\"\n413.     done\n414. }\n415. \n416. initialize_sudo() {\n417.     if [[ \"$EUID\" -eq 0 ]]; then\n418.         SUDO=()\n419.         return\n420.     fi\n421. \n422.     log \"sudo is required to install system packages, Node.js, global npm tools, and the pm2 boot service.\"\n423.     sudo -v\n424.     SUDO=(sudo)\n425. }\n426. \n427. resolve_run_user() {\n428.     if [[ -n \"${SUDO_USER:-}\" && \"${SUDO_USER:-}\" != \"root\" ]]; then\n429. }\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes a select query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeSelect(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const selectedColumns = parseSelectedColumns(this.selectedColumns);\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const orderByClause = queryValues) : null;\n+\n+        if (this.selectOptions.head) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                count,\n+                status:              .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>, count);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an insert query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeInsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const rows = this.mutationRows.map((row) => stripUndefinedValues(row));\n+        const columnNames = uniqueStrings(rows.flatMap((row) => Object.keys(row)));\n+\n+        const valuesSql = rows\n+            .map((row) => {\n+                const placeholders = columnNames.map((column) =>\n+                    createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(column in row ? row[column] : null)),\n+                );\n+                return `(${placeholders.join(', ')})`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ');\n+\n+        const sql = [\n+            `INSERT INTO ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an update query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeUpdate(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const updateColumns = Object.keys(this.mutationValues);\n+\n+        if (updateColumns.length === 0) {\n+            return this.createMutationResponse([]);\n+        }\n+\n+        const assignments = updateColumns.map(\n+            (column) => `${quoteIdentifier(column)} = ${createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(this.mutationValues[column]))}`,\n+        );\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [\n+                `UPDATE ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} SET ${assignments.join(', ')}`,\n+                whereClause,\n+                this.createReturningClause(),\n+            ]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes a delete query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeDelete(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const result = await         })\n+            .join(', ');\n+        const updateColumns = columnNames.filter((column) => column !== 'id');\n+        const updateAssignments = updateColumns.map(\n+            (column) => (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            onConflictClause,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string,                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse([...rows], null);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Applies single/maybeSingle response             };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0], error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.singleMode === 'maybeSingle') {\n+ */\n+    private createOrderByClause(): string {\n+        if (this.orders.length === 0) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `ORDER BY ${this.orders\n+            .map((order) (this.offsetCount === null) {\n+            return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)}`;\n+        }\n+\n+        return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)} OFFSET Creates SQL for one simple filter.\n+ */\n+function createFilterCondition(filter: LocalPostgresFilter, values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+    const column = quoteIdentifier(filter.column);\n+ 'not-is', value });\n+        } else if (operator === 'eq') {\n+            this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'neq', value });\n+        }\n+\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     *        this.singleMode = 'single';\n+        return this.execute();\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Marks the query as requiring at most one row.\n+     */\n+    public maybeSingle(): */\n+    public abortSignal(signal: AbortSignal): this {\n+        this.signal = signal;\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Makes the query builder awaitable.\n+     */\n+    public }\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes a select query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeSelect(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const selectedColumns =              .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>,         return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const .join(', ');\n+\n+        const sql = [\n+            `INSERT INTO ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+ private async executeUpsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            onConflictClause,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse([...rows], null);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Applies single/maybeSingle response             };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0], error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.singleMode === 'maybeSingle') {\n+ 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Counts rows matching the current filters.\n+     */\n+    private async executeCount(whereClause: string, whereValues: ReadonlyArray<unknown>): */\n+    private createOrderByClause(): string {\n+        if (this.orders.length === 0) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `ORDER BY ${this.orders\n+            .map((order) Creates SQL for one simple filter.\n+ */\n+function createFilterCondition(filter: LocalPostgresFilter, values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+    const column = quoteIdentifier(filter.column);\n+    const value = normalizeDatabaseValue(filter.value);\n+\n+    switch (filter.operator) {\n+        case 'eq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NULL` : `${column} =        case 'gte':\n+            return `${column} >= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'like':\n+            return `${column} LIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE Creates SQL for one PostgREST `.or(...)` filter.\n+ */\n+function createOrFilterCondition(filter: string, values: Array<unknown>): string | null {\n+    const conditions: Array<string> = [];\n+\n+    for (const part of splitPostgrestOrFilter(filter)) {\n+        const parsedFilter = parsePostgrestFilter(part);\n+        if (!parsedFilter) {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        if       const character = filter[index]!;\n+        const previousCharacter = filter[index - 1];\n+\n+        if (character === '\"' && previousCharacter !== '\\\\') {\n+            isInsideString = ']')) {\n+            depth--;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (character === ',' && depth === 0 && !isInsideString) {\n+            parts.push(current);\n+            current = '';\n+   .map((column) => column.trim())\n+            .filter(Boolean);\n+    }\n+\n+    return DEFAULT_UPSERT_CONFLICT_COLUMNS_BY_TABLE.get(tableBaseName) || [];\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Converts driver errors });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds greater-than-or-equal filter.\n+     */\n+    public gte(column: string, value: unknown): this {\n+        this.filters.push({ column,  this.filters.push({ column, operator: 'ilike', value });\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds an OR filter in the PostgREST format used by Supabase.\n+     */\n+    public return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Adds inclusive range pagination.\n+     */\n+    public range(from: number, to: number): this {\n+        this.offsetCount = from;\n+        this.limitCount = Math.max(0, to - from + 1);\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Marks the query as requiring exactly one row.\n+     */\n+    public single(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        this.singleMode = 'single';\n+        return this.execute();\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Marks the query as requiring at most one row.\n+     */\n+    public maybeSingle(): */\n+    public abortSignal(signal: AbortSignal): this {\n+        this.signal = signal;\n+        return this;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Makes the query builder awaitable.\n+     */\n+    public configured query.\n+     */\n+    private async execute(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        try {\n+            if (this.signal?.aborted) {\n+                throw new Error('The operation was aborted.');\n+            }\n+\n+            switch (this.operation) {\n+                case 'insert':\n+                    return this.executeInsert();\n+                case 'update':\n+                    return this.executeUpdate();\n+                case 'delete':\n+                    return this.executeDelete();\n+                case 'upsert':\n+                    return this.executeUpsert();\n+                case 'select':\n+                default:\n+                    return this.executeSelect();\n+            }\n+        } catch (error) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: normalizePostgresError(error),\n+                status: 400,\n+                statusText: 'Bad Request',\n+            };\n+        }\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes a select query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeSelect(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const selectedColumns = parseSelectedColumns(this.selectedColumns);\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const orderByClause = this.createOrderByClause();\n+        const limitClause = this.createLimitClause(queryValues);\n+        const count = this.selectOptions.count === 'exact' ? await this.executeCount(whereClause, queryValues) : null;\n+\n+        if (this.selectOptions.head) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                count,\n+                status: 200,\n+                statusText: 'OK',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [\n+                `SELECT ${createSelectExpression(selectedColumns)} FROM ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)}`,\n+                whereClause,\n+                orderByClause,\n+                limitClause,\n+            ]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>, count);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an insert query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeInsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const rows = this.mutationRows.map((row) => stripUndefinedValues(row));\n+        const columnNames = uniqueStrings(rows.flatMap((row) => Object.keys(row)));\n+\n+        const valuesSql = rows\n+            .map((row) => {\n+                const placeholders = columnNames.map((column) =>\n+                    createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(column in row ? row[column] : null)),\n+                );\n+                return `(${placeholders.join(', ')})`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ');\n+\n+        const sql = [\n+            `INSERT INTO ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an update query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeUpdate(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const updateColumns = Object.keys(this.mutationValues);\n+\n+        if (updateColumns.length === 0) {\n+            return this.createMutationResponse([]);\n+        }\n+\n+        const assignments = updateColumns.map(\n+            (column) => `${quoteIdentifier(column)} = ${createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(this.mutationValues[column]))}`,\n+        );\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [\n+                `UPDATE ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} SET ${assignments.join(', ')}`,\n+                whereClause,\n+                this.createReturningClause(),\n+            ]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes a delete query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeDelete(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const whereClause = this.createWhereClause(queryValues);\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [`DELETE FROM ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)}`, whereClause, this.createReturningClause()]\n+                .filter(Boolean)\n+                .join(' '),\n+            queryValues,\n+        );\n+\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Executes an upsert query.\n+     */\n+    private async executeUpsert(): Promise<LocalPostgresQueryResult> {\n+        if (this.mutationRows.length === 0) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        const rows = this.mutationRows.map((row) => stripUndefinedValues(row));\n+        const columnNames = uniqueStrings(rows.flatMap((row) => Object.keys(row)));\n+        const conflictColumns = resolveUpsertConflictColumns(resolveTableBaseName(this.tableName), this.upsertOptions);\n+        const queryValues: Array<unknown> = [];\n+        const valuesSql = rows\n+            .map((row) => {\n+                const placeholders = columnNames.map((column) =>\n+                    createPlaceholder(queryValues, normalizeDatabaseValue(column in row ? row[column] : null)),\n+                );\n+                return `(${placeholders.join(', ')})`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ');\n+        const updateColumns = columnNames.filter((column) => column !== 'id');\n+        const updateAssignments = updateColumns.map(\n+            (column) => `${quoteIdentifier(column)} = EXCLUDED.${quoteIdentifier(column)}`,\n+        );\n+\n+        const onConflictClause =\n+            conflictColumns.length === 0\n+                ? ''\n+                : updateAssignments.length === 0\n+                  ? `ON CONFLICT (${conflictColumns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')}) DO NOTHING`\n+                  : `ON CONFLICT (${conflictColumns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')}) DO UPDATE SET ${updateAssignments.join(', ')}`;\n+\n+        const sql = [\n+            `INSERT INTO ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)} (${columnNames.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ')})`,\n+            `VALUES ${valuesSql}`,\n+            onConflictClause,\n+            this.createReturningClause(),\n+        ]\n+            .filter(Boolean)\n+            .join(' ');\n+\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(sql, queryValues);\n+        return this.createMutationResponse(result.rows as Array<Record<string, unknown>>);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates a mutation response, optionally loading selected mutated rows.\n+     */\n+    private createMutationResponse(rows: ReadonlyArray<Record<string, unknown>>): LocalPostgresQueryResult {\n+        if (!this.isReturningSelection) {\n+            return {\n+                data: null,\n+                error: null,\n+                status: 201,\n+                statusText: 'Created',\n+            };\n+        }\n+\n+        return this.finalizeDataResponse([...rows], null);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Applies single/maybeSingle response semantics.\n+     */\n+    private finalizeDataResponse(data: Array<Record<string, unknown>>, count: number | null): LocalPostgresQueryResult {\n+        if (this.singleMode === 'single') {\n+            if (data.length !== 1) {\n+                return {\n+                    data: null,\n+                    error: {\n+                        code: 'PGRST116',\n+                        message: `Expected exactly one row, received ${data.length}.`,\n+                    },\n+                    count,\n+                    status: 406,\n+                    statusText: 'Not Acceptable',\n+                };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0], error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.singleMode === 'maybeSingle') {\n+            if (data.length > 1) {\n+                return {\n+                    data: null,\n+                    error: {\n+                        code: 'PGRST116',\n+                        message: `Expected zero or one row, received ${data.length}.`,\n+                    },\n+                    count,\n+                    status: 406,\n+                    statusText: 'Not Acceptable',\n+                };\n+            }\n+\n+            return { data: data[0] || null, error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+        }\n+\n+        return { data, error: null, count, status: 200, statusText: 'OK' };\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Counts rows matching the current filters.\n+     */\n+    private async executeCount(whereClause: string, whereValues: ReadonlyArray<unknown>): Promise<number> {\n+        const result = await this.pool.query(\n+            [`SELECT COUNT(*)::INT AS \"count\" FROM ${quoteIdentifier(this.tableName)}`, whereClause].filter(Boolean).join(' '),\n+            [...whereValues],\n+        );\n+\n+        return Number(result.rows[0]?.count || 0);\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL WHERE clause.\n+     */\n+    private createWhereClause(values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+        const parts: Array<string> = [];\n+\n+        for (const filter of this.filters) {\n+            parts.push(createFilterCondition(filter, values));\n+        }\n+\n+        for (const filter of this.orFilters) {\n+            const condition = createOrFilterCondition(filter, values);\n+            if (condition) {\n+                parts.push(condition);\n+            }\n+        }\n+\n+        return parts.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${parts.join(' AND ')}` : '';\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL ORDER BY clause.\n+     */\n+    private createOrderByClause(): string {\n+        if (this.orders.length === 0) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `ORDER BY ${this.orders\n+            .map((order) => {\n+                const direction = order.ascending ? 'ASC' : 'DESC';\n+                const nullOrder =\n+                    order.nullsFirst === true ? ' NULLS FIRST' : order.nullsFirst === false ? ' NULLS LAST' : '';\n+                return `${quoteIdentifier(order.column)} ${direction}${nullOrder}`;\n+            })\n+            .join(', ')}`;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL LIMIT/OFFSET clause.\n+     */\n+    private createLimitClause(values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+        if (this.limitCount === null) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        if (this.offsetCount === null) {\n+            return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)}`;\n+        }\n+\n+        return `LIMIT ${createPlaceholder(values, this.limitCount)} OFFSET ${createPlaceholder(values, this.offsetCount)}`;\n+    }\n+\n+    /**\n+     * Creates the SQL RETURNING clause for mutation queries.\n+     */\n+    private createReturningClause(): string {\n+        if (!this.isReturningSelection) {\n+            return '';\n+        }\n+\n+        return `RETURNING ${createSelectExpression(parseSelectedColumns(this.selectedColumns))}`;\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates SQL for one simple filter.\n+ */\n+function createFilterCondition(filter: LocalPostgresFilter, values: Array<unknown>): string {\n+    const column = quoteIdentifier(filter.column);\n+    const value = normalizeDatabaseValue(filter.value);\n+\n+    switch (filter.operator) {\n+        case 'eq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NULL` : `${column} = ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'neq':\n+            return value === null ? `${column} IS NOT NULL` : `${column} <> ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'is':\n+            return filter.value === null ? `${column} IS NULL` : `${column} IS NOT DISTINCT FROM ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'not-is':\n+            return filter.value === null ? `${column} IS NOT NULL` : `${column} IS DISTINCT FROM ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'in': {\n+            const normalizedValues = Array.isArray(filter.value) ? filter.value.map(normalizeDatabaseValue) : [];\n+            if (normalizedValues.length === 0) {\n+                return 'FALSE';\n+            }\n+\n+            return `${column} IN (${normalizedValues.map((normalizedValue) => createPlaceholder(values, normalizedValue)).join(', ')})`;\n+        }\n+        case 'lt':\n+            return `${column} < ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'lte':\n+            return `${column} <= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'gt':\n+            return `${column} > ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'gte':\n+            return `${column} >= ${createPlaceholder(values, value)}`;\n+        case 'like':\n+            return `${column} LIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE '\\\\'`;\n+        case 'ilike':\n+            return `${column} ILIKE ${createPlaceholder(values, value)} ESCAPE '\\\\'`;\n+        default:\n+            return 'TRUE';\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates SQL for one PostgREST `.or(...)` filter.\n+ */\n+function createOrFilterCondition(filter: string, values: Array<unknown>): string | null {\n+    const conditions: Array<string> = [];\n+\n+    for (const part of splitPostgrestOrFilter(filter)) {\n+        const parsedFilter = parsePostgrestFilter(part);\n+        if (!parsedFilter) {\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (parsedFilter.operator === 'cs') {\n+            conditions.push('FALSE');\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        conditions.push(\n+            createFilterCondition(\n+                {\n+                    column: parsedFilter.column,\n+                    operator: normalizePostgrestOperator(parsedFilter.operator),\n+                    value: decodePostgrestFilterValue(parsedFilter.value),\n+                },\n+                values,\n+            ),\n+        );\n+    }\n+\n+    if (conditions.length === 0) {\n+        return null;\n+    }\n+\n+    return `(${conditions.join(' OR ')})`;\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Splits a PostgREST OR filter while keeping JSON literals intact.\n+ */\n+function splitPostgrestOrFilter(filter: string): Array<string> {\n+    const parts: Array<string> = [];\n+    let current = '';\n+    let depth = 0;\n+    let isInsideString = false;\n+\n+    for (let index = 0; index < filter.length; index++) {\n+        const character = filter[index]!;\n+        const previousCharacter = filter[index - 1];\n+\n+        if (character === '\"' && previousCharacter !== '\\\\') {\n+            isInsideString = !isInsideString;\n+        } else if (!isInsideString && (character === '{' || character === '[')) {\n+            depth++;\n+        } else if (!isInsideString && (character === '}' || character === ']')) {\n+            depth--;\n+        }\n+\n+        if (character === ',' && depth === 0 && !isInsideString) {\n+            parts.push(current);\n+            current = '';\n+            continue;\n+        }\n+\n+        current += character;\n+    }\n+\n+    if (current) {\n+        parts.push(current);\n+    }\n+\n+    return parts.map((part) => part.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parsed PostgREST filter expression.\n+ */\n+type ParsedPostgrestFilter = {\n+    readonly column: string;\n+    readonly operator: string;\n+    readonly value: string;\n+};\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parses one PostgREST filter expression.\n+ */\n+function parsePostgrestFilter(filter: string): ParsedPostgrestFilter | null {\n+    const match = /^([^.]*)\\.([a-z]+)\\.([\\s\\S]*)$/iu.exec(filter.trim());\n+    if (!match) {\n+        return null;\n+    }\n+\n+    return {\n+        column: match[1]!,\n+        operator: match[2]!.toLowerCase(),\n+        value: match[3]!,\n+    };\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Converts PostgREST operators into internal filter operators.\n+ */\n+function normalizePostgrestOperator(operator: string): LocalPostgresFilter['operator'] {\n+    switch (operator) {\n+        case 'neq':\n+            return 'neq';\n+        case 'ilike':\n+            return 'ilike';\n+        case 'like':\n+            return 'like';\n+        case 'lt':\n+            return 'lt';\n+        case 'lte':\n+            return 'lte';\n+        case 'gt':\n+            return 'gt';\n+        case 'gte':\n+            return 'gte';\n+        case 'eq':\n+        default:\n+            return 'eq';\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Decodes a PostgREST filter value when URL-encoded by callers.\n+ */\n+function decodePostgrestFilterValue(value: string): string {\n+    try {\n+        return decodeURIComponent(value);\n+    } catch {\n+        return value;\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Creates a select expression from parsed columns.\n+ */\n+function createSelectExpression(columns: ReadonlyArray<string>): string {\n+    if (columns.length === 0 || columns.includes('*')) {\n+        return '*';\n+    }\n+\n+    return columns.map(quoteIdentifier).join(', ');\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Parses a simple Supabase select column list.\n+ */\n+function parseSelectedColumns(columns: string): Array<string> {\n+    const trimmedColumns = columns.trim();\n+    if (!trimmedColumns || trimmedColumns === '*') {\n+        return ['*'];\n+    }\n+\n+    return trimmedColumns\n+        .split(',')\n+        .map((column) => column.trim())\n+        .filter(Boolean)\n+        .map((column) => column.split(':').pop() || column)\n+        .map((column) => column.replace(/\\s+/g, ''))\n+        .filter((column) => /^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$/u.test(column));\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Converts mutation payloads into a uniform array of records.\n+ */\n+function normalizeMutationRows(values: TODO_any): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {\n+    if (Array.isArray(values)) {\n+        return values.map((row) => stripUndefinedValues(row || {}));\n+    }\n+\n+    return [stripUndefinedValues(values || {})];\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Removes undefined values because Supabase omits them from mutation payloads.\n+ */\n+function stripUndefinedValues(values: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> {\n+    const result: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n+\n+    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(values)) {\n+        if (value !== undefined) {\n+            result[key] = value;\n+        }\n+    }\n+\n+    return result;\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Resolves a table base name from the actual prefixed table name.\n+ */\n+function resolveTableBaseName(tableName: string): string {\n+    const knownTableNames = uniqueStrings([...Array.from(DEFAULT_UPSERT_CONFLICT_COLUMNS_BY_TABLE.keys()), '_Server']).sort(\n+        (left, right) => right.length - left.length,\n+    );\n+\n+    return knownTableNames.find((knownTableName) => tableName.endsWith(knownTableName)) || tableName;\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Resolves upsert conflict columns.\n+ */\n+function resolveUpsertConflictColumns(\n+    tableBaseName: string,\n+    options: LocalPostgresUpsertOptions,\n+): ReadonlyArray<string> {\n+    if (options.onConflict) {\n+        return options.onConflict\n+            .split(',')\n+            .map((column) => column.trim())\n+            .filter(Boolean);\n+    }\n+\n+    return DEFAULT_UPSERT_CONFLICT_COLUMNS_BY_TABLE.get(tableBaseName) || [];\n+}\n+\n+/**\n+ * Converts driver errors
